symptom: AVD (Azure Virtual Desktop) session hosts cannot be enrolled to Intune in Mooncake (21Vianet); the enrollment option is absent in the Intune portal
rootCause: Intune management for AVD Azure AD-joined VMs is only supported in Azure Public and Azure Government clouds; Mooncake 21v does not support this feature. Feature #15571094 is being tracked by PG. Manually re-joining the VM to Azure AD to trigger auto-enrollment has been tested but is NOT officially supported.
solution: No officially supported enrollment path for AVD in 21v as of 2023-05. Workaround (unsupported): (1) Ensure Intune auto-enrollment is enabled; (2) In AVD session host, disconnect from Azure AD; (3) Re-join Azure AD to trigger auto-enrollment. Personal pool hosts cannot be manually enrolled. Monitor Feature #15571094 for GA.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/Azure AD-joined VMs/With Intune.md

symptom: In dual-federation environment (same UPN in 21v and Global Azure AD), iOS PowerApps shows 'Org Data Removal – Your organization has removed its data associated with this app'; Outlook access issues when wrong cloud credential is selected in Authenticator
rootCause: Intune SDK for iOS v16.0.0 (released 2022/05/25) changed device primary user identification from UPN to OID. In dual-fed environments where users have the same UPN in both 21v and Global, the Intune MAM incorrectly associates apps with the wrong cloud tenant and triggers data removal. All Microsoft SaaS apps on iOS with the same Global account are impacted.
solution: Short-term: (1) Create separate Edge web apps for PowerApps 21v and Global (uninstall native PowerApps app); (2) Create Teams add-in apps for PowerApps 21v and Global. Long-term: (1) Change user UPN to be different between clouds via ADFS config/AAD Connect sync rules; (2) Wait for 'Multiple Manage Account support for MAM' feature (ETA unknown); (3) Use cross-cloud B2B user to access 21v PowerApps when PowerApps PG supports it.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Case study/Dual-fed with Intune compatibility.md

symptom: Android DA device reported Noncompliant with rules: SecurityBlockJailbrokenDevices (rooted device), SecurityRequireSafetyNetAttestationBasicIntegrity=False, SecurityRequireSafetyNetAttestationCertifiedDevice=False
rootCause: Device is rooted OR SafetyNet attestation is failing — device does not pass Google's basic integrity or certified device check. In China, SafetyNet depends on Google Mobile Services (GMS) which is not available in Mooncake/21v environment.
solution: Use Kusto: `CalculationQueueLibrary | where deviceId == '{deviceId}' | project env_time, EventId, message, complianceState, complianceDetails` to confirm noncompliant rules. For SafetyNet in 21v: SafetyNet is unsupported due to GMS dependency — remove SafetyNetAttestationBasicIntegrity and SafetyNetAttestationCertifiedDevice requirements from compliance policies. For truly rooted devices, device must be restored to factory settings.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/Android Compliance policy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Android LOB (.apk) app download fails via Intune Company Portal on cellular network; 'download failed' error shown; OMADM log shows HTTP 499 status code during ContentService download
rootCause: HTTP 499 means the client disconnected before the server completed the response. Cellular network instability in China mainland causes the download connection to drop during large APK download. The retry mechanism eventually succeeds but can take 10-15 minutes on repeated attempts.
solution: Use corporate Wi-Fi network for LOB app installation to avoid network instability. On cellular: after clicking 'Install' in Company Portal, manually click the 'Sync' button to trigger an immediate retry of the download. Kusto: `HttpSubsystem | where url contains '{deviceId}' | project env_time, statusCode, url` to confirm HTTP status codes.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/Android LOB App in China mainalnd.md

symptom: OneDrive/Outlook for Android frequently signs out or is unable to sign in; Outlook log shows 'Unable to access the network due to power optimizations' and 'BROKER_AUTHENTICATOR_NOT_RESPONDING'
rootCause: Microsoft Authenticator (when used as broker app) is not in the Android battery optimization exclusion list. Android kills Authenticator due to power optimization settings, so Outlook/OneDrive cannot obtain refreshed tokens through the broker. Company Portal maintains tokens correctly because it is designed for this role.
solution: Uninstall Microsoft Authenticator app; let Company Portal serve as the authentication broker app. After uninstalling Authenticator, verify issue disappears. Alternative: add Authenticator to battery optimization exclusion list (Settings > Battery > Not optimized). Debug log collection: enable Android debug logs to confirm which broker app (Authenticator vs Company Portal) is being used.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/OneDrive_Outlook for Android sign out and sometime.md

symptom: MAM enrollment fails with error 'application is already MDM enrolled and the provided identity does not match the MDM enrolled identity'; user cannot enroll into managed app with their account
rootCause: The managed app (e.g., Outlook) was previously enrolled by a different user. The Intune MAM service checks that the enrolling identity (UPN/OID hash) matches the existing MDM-enrolled identity. A mismatch blocks the new enrollment. Common after shared device handover without factory reset.
solution: Option 1: Factory reset the device so the previous MAM enrollment is fully cleared. Option 2: Have the previous user properly unenroll via Company Portal (Devices -> Remove this device), confirm managed apps are removed, then re-enroll as the new user. Log keyword: 'CMAREnrollmentPreReqOperation' and 'does not match the MDM enrolled identity'.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/MAM Log Analysis- Key word.md

symptom: Outlook for Android sign-in fails or is blocked when Conditional Access (CA) policy requires MAM (App Protection Policy) compliance; user cannot access corporate email
rootCause: CA policy with MAM requirement has two prerequisites: (1) Company Portal app must be installed on the Android device, (2) Device must be AAD-registered. If either is missing, the MAM enrollment pre-check fails and CA blocks access.
solution: Ensure Android device meets MAM requirements: (1) Install Microsoft Intune Company Portal; (2) Register device with Azure AD (via Company Portal setup or manual AAD registration). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/app-protection-policy#app-protection-policies-for-microsoft-365-office-apps
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/MAM for Android requirement - Outlook for Android.md

symptom: iOS Intune-enrolled devices receive App Protection Policy with type 'Apps on unmanaged devices' instead of 'Apps on Intune managed devices'; MAM managed/unmanaged device targeting does not work correctly
rootCause: For iOS, apps must be configured with 'IntuneMAMUPN' app configuration key to be recognized as managed by Intune MAM SDK. Without this key, even if the device is MDM-enrolled, the MAM SDK treats apps as unmanaged. Apps must also be deployed via Intune and installed through Company Portal (not App Store directly).
solution: 1. Create App Configuration Policy (Managed devices, iOS). Add keys: IntuneMAMUPN={{UserPrincipalName}}, IntuneMAMDeviceID={{deviceID}} for third-party/LOB apps. 2. Deploy the app via Intune. 3. For already-installed apps: Company Portal -> Apps -> Install -> accept 'App Management Change' prompt to transfer to managed state.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/MAM policy for managed iOS devices.md

symptom: Intune MAM selective wipe fails on Android device; Company Portal does not wake up to perform wipe; corporate data not removed after wipe command issued from Intune portal
rootCause: Android auto-start permission is disabled for Company Portal. Android power management kills the Company Portal background service, preventing it from receiving and executing the selective wipe command from Intune service.
solution: Enable auto-start permission for Company Portal in Android device settings (Settings -> Apps -> Company Portal -> Battery -> Allow auto-start or Background activity). Debug check: compare Company Portal debug logs - missing 'awakened' entries indicates auto-start is disabled. After enabling, wipe operation completes successfully.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/MAM selective wipe the App.md

symptom: Outlook for iOS shows 'Misconfiguration Alert' and new user cannot set up corporate email on a device previously used by another user; diagnostic log shows 'MDM UPN Mismatch'
rootCause: When a device is transferred to a new user without factory reset, the previous user's MAM enrollment is not fully removed. Outlook for iOS retains the old device ID for ~1 hour after new user enrolls. MAM service detects device ID mismatch and blocks app setup. Root cause: incomplete MAM selective wipe from previous user (ICM 174190862).
solution: Option 1 (Recommended): Factory reset before device handover. Option 2: (1) Previous user unenrolls from Company Portal; (2) Confirm managed apps removed; (3) New user enrolls via Company Portal; (4) Wait for managed apps to deploy; (5) Check device compliance in Company Portal. Log check: 'MDM UPN Mismatch' in Outlook diagnostic log; verify mamenrollmentoperation funnelKey shows correct new device ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/Outlook unable to detect the newly enrolled device.md

symptom: iOS managed app config policy (App Configuration Profile) not applied or takes 10-20 minutes to reach the device; after app reinstall, Intune portal shows app config policy as compliant but config never delivers to the iOS device; Exchange on-prem mailbox with no hybrid auth, Android app config works but iOS never works
rootCause: App config delivery timing is delayed by MDM policy sync cycle; initial enrollment may capture old DeviceID before device re-registers with Intune service. IntuneEvent shows AppConfig_ConfigDictionary does not exist on device then remediation command issued, but delivery lags until next device check-in. In Exchange on-prem without hybrid auth, additional auth issues may prevent iOS from applying config.
solution: 1. Use Kusto IntuneEvent to verify: IntuneEvent | where DeviceId == '{deviceId}' | where Col1 contains 'AppConfig' | project env_time, UserId, DeviceId, Col1, Col2 | order by env_time asc. 2. Check DeviceEG and UserEG policy baseline Compliance state. 3. Trigger manual sync from Intune portal and wait 15-20 min. 4. Also run DeviceManagementProvider | where userId == '{userId}' | project env_time, userId, DeviceID=ActivityId, PolicyName=name, Compliance=reportComplianceState, message. 5. For Exchange on-prem: verify hybrid modern auth is configured.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/iOS_Android App config.md

symptom: Customer requests Microsoft Tunnel VPN configuration via 21v Intune; Tunnel gateway or Tunnel configuration options not available in 21v Intune portal; VPN per-app profiles using Tunnel cannot be deployed
rootCause: Microsoft Tunnel requires Microsoft Defender for Endpoint integration which is unavailable in 21v. Additionally, China law restricts VPN services to the three major licensed telecom operators (China Mobile/Unicom/Telecom), preventing Microsoft from operating Tunnel VPN infrastructure in mainland China.
solution: Use third-party VPN apps deployed via Intune App Protection or Per-App VPN profiles (e.g., Cisco AnyConnect, Palo Alto GlobalProtect). For Windows devices: Always-On VPN via Azure VPN Gateway with IKEv2 can be configured via Device Configuration > VPN profile. Do not set customer expectations for Tunnel availability in 21v as there is no roadmap.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: Customer cannot set up Android Enterprise (Work Profile, Fully Managed/COBO, COPE, or Dedicated/COSU) enrollment in 21v Intune; Android Enterprise enrollment types not available in 21v portal; BYOD Android devices cannot be enrolled with Work Profile separation
rootCause: Android Enterprise requires Google Mobile Services (GMS) specifically Google Play Protect and Google Device Policy which are unavailable in China. 21v Intune does not support any Android Enterprise management types due to this GMS dependency.
solution: Use Android Device Administrator (DA) management mode instead. 21v Intune supports Android DA enrollment including Android 15. For BYOD configure Android DA enrollment with Company Portal. For app distribution without Google Play use LOB .apk app deployment (see onenote-android-app-store-china.md guide). For managed app-only scenarios use MAM without enrollment. For AOSP-based devices monitor Feature 14514386.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: Cannot deploy Microsoft Store (new) apps or winget-sourced application packages from 21v Intune; Microsoft Store app (new) type not available in Intune Apps section in 21v portal; Windows Package Manager (winget) deployment not working in 21v
rootCause: Microsoft Store (new) app type relies on Windows Package Manager (winget). Microsoft confirmed in 2020 that new Microsoft Store will not be supported for Mooncake due to regulatory compliance concerns with winget in China (DCR-M365-5114). This remains unsupported as of 2026.
solution: Use Win32 app packaging (.intunewin format) for all custom Windows app deployments in 21v. Use Microsoft 365 Apps deployment type for Office. For Windows built-in feature management use OMA-URI/CSP configurations. Avoid winget-based deployments. Monitor DCR-M365-5114 for potential future support.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: Windows Update for Business (WUfB) Feature Update policies and Quality Update rings configured in 21v Intune portal have no effect on managed Windows devices; Windows Update for Business Reports not showing data in 21v
rootCause: Windows Feature & Quality Update management through Intune is not available in 21v; dev team confirmed no plan as of 2024-06 (DCR-M365-4825). Windows Update for Business Report is also unavailable (DCR-M365-6907). Update ring policies may appear configurable but do not execute.
solution: Use WSUS (Windows Server Update Services) for all Windows update management in 21v environments. Alternatively deploy third-party patch management solutions. For update compliance reporting create custom reports using Kusto DeviceComplianceOrg or IntuneDevices tables. Monitor DCR-M365-4825 for future WUfB support.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: SCCM co-managed devices in 21v cannot receive SCCM policies or check-in with management point when off-premises; Cloud Management Gateway (CMG) deployment to Azure 21v fails or option is unavailable in SCCM console
rootCause: Azure Cloud Management Gateway (CMG) service is not available in 21v Azure environment. Without CMG, SCCM CmClient can only authenticate with local on-premises AD and communicate with on-prem SCCM management point. Remote co-managed devices lose SCCM connectivity when off VPN.
solution: Only Hybrid AAD Join + co-management is supported in 21v. Ensure devices have VPN or direct line-of-sight to on-premises AD/SCCM infrastructure. Configure Always-On VPN for remote workers so SCCM workloads can process. For workloads needing cloud management move them from SCCM to Intune. Collect CCM logs at C:\Windows\CCM\Logs for co-management troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: Customer cannot use Intune Cloud PKI (cloud-based Certificate Authority) for certificate provisioning in 21v; Intune Suite add-on or Cloud PKI option not available in 21v Intune admin center
rootCause: Cloud PKI requires the Intune Suite or Cloud PKI add-on license which is not available for 21v Intune. Feature 27029020 is tracking Mooncake support but has no confirmed timeline.
solution: Use on-premises ADCS (Active Directory Certificate Services) with NDES role and Intune Certificate Connector for SCEP-based certificate deployment. Steps: 1) Deploy NDES server + Intune Certificate Connector; 2) Create SCEP certificate profile in Intune; 3) Assign to device/user groups. For Trusted Root certificates use Trusted Certificate profile in Intune. Monitor Feature 27029020 for Cloud PKI 21v availability.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## China Intune Feature gaps.md

symptom: Android device marked Noncompliant due to USB debugging enabled; Company Portal reports Block USB debugging on device rule violation; OMA-DM log shows InstanceSource: ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/DeviceLock/DevicePolicyManager/IsUsbDebuggingAllowedTitle
rootCause: Company Portal checks USB debugging via Android global Settings adb_enabled key (integer: 0=disabled, 1=enabled). If device developer options has USB debugging ON, Intune compliance policy with Block USB debugging will report noncompliant. For device encryption noncompliance: getEncryptionStatus() returning 4 means ENCRYPTION_STATUS_ACTIVE_DEFAULT_KEY - device is encrypted but startup PIN is set, causing wrong status reporting.
solution: USB debugging: Disable Developer Options or turn off USB debugging via Settings > Developer options > USB debugging. Encryption value 4 (ENCRYPTION_STATUS_ACTIVE_DEFAULT_KEY): Remove the startup/boot PIN from device encryption settings. Kusto: CalculationQueueLibrary | where deviceId == '{deviceId}' | project env_time, EventId, message, complianceState, complianceDetails to see per-setting compliance values. OMA-DM log shows the exact API path and returned value for each compliance check.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/Android compliance API.md

symptom: Android device cannot enroll, app protection policies not applying, or Intune-managed apps (Outlook/OneDrive) behaving abnormally; need to collect diagnostic logs for troubleshooting
rootCause: Common pre-requisite issues: battery optimization killing Intune/Outlook/Company Portal background processes; app Launch setting not allowing auto-start; Data Saver restricting app network access; notifications disabled preventing policy push delivery.
solution: Pre-request actions before collecting logs: (1) Disable battery optimization for Outlook and Company Portal; (2) Change Launch setting for both apps from Automatic to Manage Manually, enable Auto-launch + Secondary launch + Run in background; (3) If Data Saver enabled, allow unrestricted data for Outlook and CP; (4) Enable notifications for both apps. Log collection: Company Portal: Menu > Help > Email Support > generate log, share incident ID. Outlook: Help > Collect Diagnostic log > Upload log > share incident ID. Android debug log: Enable Developer Options (tap Build Number 7x), enable USB debugging, reproduce issue with CP, then Settings > Developer options > Take bug report.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/Log collect & Android device pre-request.md

symptom: MAM app protection policy not enforced on Android device; policy check fails intermittently or app is not receiving wipe/policy commands despite MAM being configured; Company Portal debug log shows no wake-up events for the app
rootCause: Android manufacturer auto-start / auto-launch setting is disabled for Company Portal or the MAM-protected app. When auto-launch is disabled, the app is not woken up to receive and enforce MAM policy updates. The debug log will show zero wake-up events for the problematic device vs. successful wake-up counts on a healthy device.
solution: Enable auto-start / auto-launch for Company Portal (and MAM-protected apps if applicable): Settings > Apps > Company Portal > Auto-launch > Enable. After enabling, the wake-up events will appear in debug log and MAM policy enforcement resumes. Diagnostic: Compare Company Portal debug logs between affected device (no wake events) and healthy device (has wake count entries) to confirm the root cause.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/MAM selective wipe the App.md

symptom: iOS Outlook app config policy shows Succeeded in Intune portal but settings are not delivered to the device (e.g. email profile, BasicAuth settings not pre-filled); re-installing app fixes portal status but config still not applied on client
rootCause: App config delivery relies on device check-in and OMA-DM policy processing. After re-install, device creates new Device ID (MAMSharedCache: No Device ID found in keychain) causing new enrollment flow. If IntuneEvent Kusto shows Setting AppConfig_ConfigDictionary Remediation command issued=True of type Add but client never receives it, there may be timing delay or UserEG vs DeviceEG targeting mismatch.
solution: 1) Allow 10-20 minutes after enrollment or app reinstall for app config to propagate. 2) Verify targeting: Kusto IntuneEvent | where DeviceId == '{deviceId}' - UserEG-targeted policy (BL_PL_{UserEG}) should show required items > 0, DeviceEG should show compliance. 3) If config never arrives, check that app config policy uses Device enrollment channel for MDM-enrolled iOS (not MAM-WE). 4) Trigger manual sync: Settings > General > VPN & Device Management > Management Profile > Try Again. 5) If still failing escalate with IntuneEvent + DeviceManagementProvider Kusto output.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/iOS_Android App config.md

symptom: Android users cannot access SharePoint Online or Exchange Online via browser after Intune device-based Conditional Access is enabled; browser reports device not compliant or not recognized even though device is enrolled
rootCause: On Android, browser-based Conditional Access requires the Enable Browser Access feature in Company Portal to be explicitly activated. Without this, the browser cannot present the device identity certificate to AAD. Edge additionally requires user to sign in with Work/School account - without this, device information in the CA evaluation token is empty.
solution: Android Chrome: (1) Open Company Portal > triple-dot menu > Settings > Enable Browser Access; (2) Sign out of Office 365 in Chrome; (3) Restart Chrome; (4) On first access to SharePoint/Exchange, install the certificate when prompted. Android Edge: Sign in with Work or School account (Profile icon > Add Work account). Fiddler trace will show empty device_id claim until browser access certificate is installed.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Android/when you enable Device Based Access for SharePoint.md

symptom: Need to understand Intune app deployment architecture to troubleshoot why a required app is not installing, stuck downloading, or reporting incorrect status in portal
rootCause: Intune uses two parallel deployment architectures: (1) Main OMA-DM/IWS path for Store apps and LOB packages (.apk/.ipa/.msi/.appx/.msix) - push notification triggers device check-in, DownloadService_Download handles content, DeviceManagementProvider tracks status via GetAppStatus/ReportAppStatus; (2) SideCar/IME path via StatelessSideCarGatewayService for PowerShell scripts and Win32 apps (.intunewin) - runs as separate Windows service, impersonates logged-in user to retrieve MDM cert from local machine personal store.
solution: Store/LOB apps: trace push notification > OMA-DM check-in > DownloadService > GetAppStatus/ReportAppStatus flow. DeviceManagementProvider Kusto table tracks all stages. Win32/PowerShell (IME/SideCar): (1) IME agent deploys first via EnterpriseDesktopAppManagement CSP; (2) Agent polls StatelessSideCarGatewayService for deployments; (3) All install/download/status handled by agent independently. Autopilot/OOBE: device-context apps (LocURI starts .device) deploy during ESP Device Preparation under defaultuser0; user-context apps (LocURI starts .user) deploy during Account Setup under actual user. Ref: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4537739
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/App deployment structure.md

symptom: Unknown process is modifying environment variables (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment) on Intune-managed Windows device; PATH or system variables change unexpectedly after policy sync
rootCause: Windows does not natively audit registry key modifications. Without explicit audit configuration, it is impossible to identify whether Intune policy, IME agent, a Win32 app install script, or another process is responsible for unexpected environment variable changes.
solution: Enable registry audit: (1) gpedit.msc > Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Audit Policy: set Audit object access = Success and Audit process tracking = Success; (2) Run gpupdate /force; (3) HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment > right-click > Permissions > Advanced > Auditing > Add: Principal=Everyone, Type=Success (all access types); (4) After issue reproduces, check Windows Security event log for registry change events showing the responsible process and user account.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Audit registry key.md

symptom: Intune Win32 app or LOB app download fails silently on Windows; BITS (Background Intelligent Transfer Service) suspected as cause; app deployment stuck or S-1-5-18 credential rejection visible in MDMAppInstaller.exe
rootCause: BITS handles background package downloads for Intune LOB and Win32 apps on Windows. BITS failures manifest as system-account (S-1-5-18) credential rejection or network-level errors during DownloadContent() phase. Without ETL traces these errors are not visible in standard Intune logs.
solution: Collect BITS ETL + netmon + procmon + TTT traces: (1) Download netmon and procmon (Sysinternals); (2) Start TTT: TTTracer.exe -initialize then Tttracer.exe -ring -Maxfile 1024 -attach <IME_PID>; (3) Start BITS ETL: logman create trace admin_services -ow -o c:\admin_services.etl -p Microsoft-Windows-Bits-Client 0xffffffffffffffff 0xff -nb 16 16 -bs 1024 -mode Circular -f bincirc -max 4096 -ets; add providers {4A8AAA94-CFC4-46A7-8E4E-17BC45608F0A}, BITS Server Extensions, Microsoft-Windows-Bits-CompactServer, {599071ED-D475-497C-9E40-FC7283A1249B}; (4) Start netmon and procmon; (5) Trigger sync: Settings > Accounts > Access Work or School > Sync; (6) Stop all when S-1-5-18 credential rejection appears for MDMAppInstaller.exe; (7) Upload ETL + netmon + procmon + TTT to workspace for ICM escalation.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Collect BITS ETL log.md

symptom: Need to understand expected user behavior when iOS MAM app protection policy data protection settings are applied; or user reports unexpected behavior with data transfer between apps, copy/paste, Save As, PIN prompts, printing, or keyboard restrictions in managed apps like Outlook/OneDrive
rootCause: iOS MAM app protection policy has multiple data protection settings, each with sub-options that produce different user experiences. Admins and users often mistake expected MAM-enforced behavior for bugs (e.g., fewer share targets for corporate files, PIN prompt on corporate data access, inability to save to personal storage).
solution: Key MAM data protection settings and their iOS user behaviors: (1) Backup Org data to iTunes/iCloud - corporate data files are stored in a separate work storage from personal data; corporate data cannot be backed up to iTunes even when backup is enabled; (2) Send Org data to other apps - 'Policy managed apps': fewer app targets visible for corporate file transfer; sharing corporate files to non-managed apps (e.g. Apple Notes) will open the file but content cannot be read; non-corporate files show more transfer options; sub-options: 'Policy managed apps with OS sharing', 'Policy managed apps with Open-In/Share filtering', 'All apps'; (3) Receive data from other apps - 'Policy managed apps' / 'All apps' / 'All apps with incoming Org data'; Word file from Apple native app is treated as corporate data (can only be saved to work local storage); (4) Save copies of Org data (Block) - corporate data cannot be saved to personal OneDrive, personal local storage or SharePoint personal; OneDrive for Business option blocks save to local and personal storage; (5) Restrict cut/copy/paste - 'Blocked': no clipboard transfer; 'Policy managed apps': clipboard blocked to/from non-managed apps; 'Policy managed with paste in': can paste from any app INTO managed apps, but managed app cannot paste out; 'Any apps': unrestricted; (6) Printing Org data - Disable: print option hidden in managed app for corporate content; (7) Share web content - 'Policy managed browsers': web links from managed apps open only in Intune-managed browsers (Edge); (8) Block third-party keyboards - prevents non-system keyboards in managed app context; (9) Required PIN - PIN only prompted when accessing corporate data in managed apps (Work context); personal use of same app does not trigger PIN; (10) Conditional launch settings (min OS version, max PIN attempts, offline grace period, jailbreak detection, min app/SDK version) are all optional enforcement triggers. Note: All protections apply only in Work context (accessing corporate data), not personal context.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## App Protection_Configuration TSG/MAM behavior - User Experience.md

symptom: Handling Intune Sev A cases in China (Mooncake/21Vianet); need to know the most common Sev A scenarios, which Kusto queries to run, and which portals/tools to use for China environment
rootCause: Top Sev A Intune scenarios in Mooncake by frequency: (1) Device enrollment fail - APN expired/invalid, license not assigned, enrollment type mismatch, DEM user issue, ADE token issue (iOS/Mac), enrollment limit exceeded; (2) Device configuration failure - profile deployment failure, disk encryption (BitLocker) issues, compliance policy mismatch; (3) NDES/SCEP/PFX certificate issues; (4) Autopilot V2 / Windows Device Preparation - Win32 IME deployment issues during ESP; (5) Device compliance with Conditional Access.
solution: Mooncake-specific resources: Admin portal: https://intune.microsoftonline.cn/ | Company Portal: https://portal.manage.microsoftonline.cn/ | Kusto dashboard: https://dataexplorer.azure.cn/dashboards/0d518fc7-f078-48e4-b09f-297510357504 (caserush-mooncake-SCIM-Intune). Key Kusto checks by scenario - Enrollment fail: Check Intune license, DEM user, GetEnrollmentType+AccountId+EnrollmentLimit, ADE enrollment profile (iOS/Mac only), iOS Enrollment, DeviceLifecycle enrollment type, Device Enroll-renew; Config failure: Device Check-in, Device and User Effective Group, EGM membership changes, See Effective Groups a policy is targeted to, Device Configuration policy, Intune policy TattooRemoval; Compliance+CA: Check device compliance, check contents Intune writes to AAD; Autopilot V2: Autopilot v2 Kusto queries. Key external refs: APN cert setup: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/enrollment/apple-mdm-push-certificate-get | Auto-enrollment: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/enrollment/quickstart-setup-auto-enrollment | DA deprecation on Android GMS (Aug 2024): techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/intune-customer-success/microsoft-intune-ending-support-for-android-device-administrator/ba-p/3915443 | Device profile troubleshoot: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/configuration/device-profile-troubleshoot. Log collection: iOS - Company Portal log + Outlook Diagnostic log; Windows - MDM device diagnostic log + IME log.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/==[PODVM]New Hire Technical Readiness Plan==/Topic 9_ Intune - 15d/Common Sev A Readiness - Intune -5d.md

symptom: Intune portal shows HTTP 503/504 errors; device list export fails, AutoPilot CSV import returns generic 'failed to save' error, device enumeration takes excessive time or does not complete.
rootCause: Back-end service timeout: Intune service has 15,000 ms soft timeout and 30,000 ms hard failure. ScenarioTimeout/RequestTimeout exceeded causes ServiceTooBusyException. Surfaces as graph.microsoft.com HTTP 500/503/504.
solution: Collect F12/HAR trace, extract client-request-id and timestamp. Run 3 Kusto queries: (1) HttpSubsystem by ActivityId to get cVBase; (2) IntuneEvent by cV to get exception ActivityId; (3) CMService by ActivityId to confirm timeout. If confirmed, create Assistance Request in Rave/IET.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/How to troubleshoot service timeouts from the Intu.md

symptom: M365 Apps (Office 365 ProPlus) deployed as Available shows status 'unexpected' on Company Portal even though the app is installed or the installation state is correct.
rootCause: Stale or incorrect installation state stored in registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\OfficeCSP\{INSTALLATION_ID}. Company Portal reads status from this key; stale value causes unexpected status display.
solution: Backup and remove registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\OfficeCSP\{INSTALLATION_ID} to reset status, then restart the device. Reference IcM Incident-309323705.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Intune Office365 ProPlus deployment Troubleshootin.md

symptom: Intune PowerShell scripts deployed via IME always run as SYSTEM, even when 'Run this script using the logged on credentials' is enabled in policy.
rootCause: Known bug in Intune Management Extension (SideCar): user-context execution was not correctly honored. Tracked in IcM #55765826.
solution: Fixed in SideCar (IME) agent version 1.7.103 or later. Update IME agent to v1.7.103+. Verify agent version from Apps and Features.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Intune PowerShell Script deploy troubleshooting.md

symptom: PowerShell scripts deployed through Intune Management Extension (IME) do not execute on Surface Hub devices.
rootCause: Surface Hub does not support Intune Management Extension (SideCar) by design. Platform limitation.
solution: By-design limitation. Do not deploy PowerShell scripts via IME to Surface Hub. See KB4073215.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Intune PowerShell Script deploy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Intune PowerShell script deployment fails: 'The file ...ps1 is not digitally signed. You cannot run this script on the current system.' Policy result = Failed in IntuneManagementExtension.log.
rootCause: Windows PowerShell execution policy blocks unsigned scripts. IME-downloaded scripts may be treated as unsigned/remote and rejected by AllSigned or restrictive RemoteSigned policy.
solution: Check execution policy on device. IME requires Bypass or a policy permitting locally-downloaded scripts. If using 32-bit PowerShell (x86), note registry redirection may also cause issues. Verify correct bitness and execution policy.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Intune PowerShell Script deploy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Windows device enrollment fails with error 0x800706BE or 80010106. Event ID 1000 (app crash) seen in Event Viewer > Windows Logs > Application. dmenrollengine.dll fails to initialize COM.
rootCause: dmenrollengine.dll attempts COM init with COINIT_MULTITHREADED but a third-party app already initialized COM with different thread model (RPC_E_CHANGED_MODE). Known culprits in China market: CFCA FOR UKEY CSP, password agent by Hai Tai Fang Yuan (海泰方圆), Cai Kai Xin bulk system by 盟度知汇.
solution: Identify and uninstall or disable conflicting third-party UKEY or password agent software, then retry enrollment. Record additional apps matching this pattern for future reference.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Known issue/Windows Enroll failed with error 0x800706BE or 800.md

symptom: Intune portal blade (e.g., Device Configurations) fails to load or shows error. F12 trace shows backend request taking ~15 seconds then ClientClosedRequestException. Blade renders error.
rootCause: Backend microservice (e.g., StatelessDeviceConfigurationFEService) exceeds 15,000 ms ScenarioTimeout/ClientTimeout. Traffic gateway logs TimeoutException and closes connection.
solution: Collect F12 trace, extract client-request-id. Query IntuneEvent by ActivityId to find exception, then CMService by ActivityId to confirm service timeout. Once confirmed, raise ICM/Rave AR to IET team with Kusto evidence.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Blade error in the portal.md

symptom: iOS or macOS device stops receiving Intune push notifications or policy updates. Device appears to be enrolled but management commands silently fail or are never delivered.
rootCause: APNs response code 1211 (InvalidDeviceCredentials) returned by Apple Notification Relay Service. The device's MDM certificate has expired or become invalid — Intune cannot push notifications to this device via APNs.
solution: Query Kusto: `IntuneEvent | where ServiceName == "AppleNotificationRelayService" | where EventUniqueName == "DeviceNotificationResult" | where DeviceId == "<deviceId>"` — confirm Col2=="InvalidDeviceCredentials" and Col3=="1211". Device must be re-enrolled to refresh the MDM certificate. Inform user to unenroll from Company Portal then re-enroll.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Device Check-in.md

symptom: Device suddenly shows as Noncompliant in Intune portal. Compliance details contain 'Expected recent contact. Last contact: <old date>' for rule DefaultDeviceCompliancePolicy.RequireRemainContact.
rootCause: The built-in default compliance policy rule RequireRemainContact marks devices noncompliant when the device has not checked into Intune within the expected window. Common after device is offline/network-restricted for extended period.
solution: Verify via Kusto: `IntuneEvent | where ServiceName == "StatelessComplianceCalculationService" | where EventUniqueName has "ComplianceDetail" | where Col1 contains "<deviceId>"` — look for complianceState changing to Noncompliant and RuleId containing RequireRemainContact. Resolution: have user connect device to internet and sign into Company Portal to trigger check-in. Review device check-in frequency settings.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Check device compliance.md

symptom: Device unexpectedly disappears from Intune device list. Customer did not manually retire or wipe the device. No admin action found in Intune audit logs.
rootCause: Intune device cleanup rule (configured in Intune > Devices > Device cleanup rules) automatically removes devices that have not checked in for the configured number of days. Triggered by StatelessDeviceOverflowService via DeviceRemovalRuleAudit event.
solution: Confirm auto-removal in Kusto: `IntuneEvent | where ComponentName == "StatelessDeviceOverflowService" | where EventUniqueName == "DeviceRemovalRuleAudit" | where AccountId == "<accountId>" | where * has "<deviceId>"`. Advise customer to review and adjust Device Cleanup Rules in Intune portal (Devices > Device cleanup rules). Re-enroll device if needed.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Device Retire & Wipe.md

symptom: Kusto queries using DeploymentStatuses_RawData or old EffectiveGroup deployment status tables return no data or table-not-found errors when investigating policy targeting issues.
rootCause: Deployment status and Effective Group related tables were migrated from the per-MSU clusters to the centralized Intuneglobalprod cluster. Old table references (e.g., DeploymentStatuses_RawData) are no longer valid.
solution: Switch queries to MSU-specific snapshot tables on Intuneglobalprod cluster, e.g., `DeploymentStatus_Snapshot_<MSU>` or `EffectiveGroup_Snapshot_<MSU>`. Reference internal KB: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4581807. Get device/user EffectiveGroup IDs first via DeviceManagementProvider session log (look for DeviceEG and UserEG in message field).
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Device and User Effective Group.md

symptom: iPad/iOS device enrollment fails with error 'OS Version XX lower than YY'; Kusto DeviceLifecycle shows EventId 46804 with failureReason 16, SummarizedError 'OS Version XX lower than YY', ErrorCode 'EnrollmentRestrictionVersionTooLow'
rootCause: Intune enrollment restriction policy has a minimum OS version configured (e.g., iOS 16.0) and the device OS version (e.g., 15.1) is below the threshold. The ConditionalEnrollmentCheck in IOSEnrollmentService rejects the device with EnrollmentCriteriaNotMet.
solution: Check Intune portal → Devices → Enrollment Restrictions → Platform restrictions → iOS/iPadOS → Min OS version. Either lower the minimum version or update the device OS. Kusto diagnosis: (1) Query GetEnrollmentDetailsByTimeAndScaleUnitMooncake with UserId to find ActivityId and SummarizedError; (2) Query DeviceLifecycle with ActivityId for detailed failureReason and stack trace; (3) Query IntuneEvent with ActivityId for EventUniqueName 'ConditionalEnrollmentCheck' details.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Device Enrollment.md

symptom: iOS devices fail to connect to Intune endpoints or enrollment/check-in fails with SSL/TLS handshake errors; older cipher suites (e.g., DHE-RSA-CAMELLIA128-SHA) are rejected by Intune service endpoints
rootCause: Intune 21v endpoints (e.g., fef.cnpasu01.manage.microsoftonline.cn, mam.manage.microsoftonline.cn) have deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1 cipher suites. Devices using only legacy cipher suites cannot establish a secure connection.
solution: Ensure client devices support TLS 1.2 or higher with modern cipher suites. Test endpoint connectivity using testssl.sh or openssl s_client. 21v Intune endpoints list: https://learn.microsoft.com/zh-cn/mem/intune/fundamentals/china-endpoints. Key iOS-related endpoints: fef.cnbasu01.manage-ppe.microsoftonline.cn, fef.cnpasu01.manage.microsoftonline.cn, mam.manage.microsoftonline.cn, portal.manage.microsoftonline.cn, a.manage.microsoftonline.cn, m.manage.microsoftonline.cn.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/How to check SSL supported cipher suite for Intune.md

symptom: New Microsoft Teams app fails to install during Windows Autopilot ESP (Enrollment Status Page) when M365 Apps are assigned to the Autopilot device via Intune; Teams Machine-Wide Installer conflicts with other Win32 app MSI-based installs
rootCause: New Microsoft Teams uses MSI-based installation which conflicts with Win32 app installation during Autopilot ESP phase. The MSI installer cannot run concurrently with other Win32 app installations managed by Intune Management Extension.
solution: Deploy new Teams separately as a Win32 app instead of bundling with M365 Apps: (1) Download teamsbootstrapper.exe from https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2243204; (2) Download MSIX package (x64: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2196106); (3) Create install.ps1 script that copies MSIX to C:\windows	emp and runs teamsbootstrapper.exe -p -o; (4) Wrap as .intunewin; (5) Install command: powershell.exe -executionpolicy bypass -file .\install.ps1; Uninstall: teamsbootstrapper.exe -x; Detection: check C:\Program Files\WindowsApps for MSTeams_* folder.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/How to deploy new Microsoft Teams app via win32.md

symptom: Android Enterprise Work Profile passcode reset fails; OMADMLog shows 'ResetPasswordToken unknown'; Graph API returns 400 Bad Request on resetPasscode call
rootCause: The ResetPasswordToken has not been activated on the device. Android Work Profile passcode reset requires: (1) an Android Enterprise Device Restrictions profile deployed with Work Profile password required, (2) user sets the work profile passcode, (3) user authorizes company support to remotely reset password (activates the token).
solution: Deploy Android Enterprise Device Restrictions profile (Work Profile Only) with 'Require Work Profile Password' enabled. User must: set work profile passcode when prompted → accept second prompt 'Secure your Work Profile - Authorize your company support to remotely reset your work profile password' → enter passcode again. Only after token activation will resetPasscode succeed. Verify via Kusto: DeviceManagementProvider | where message contains 'ResetPasswordToken' — should show 'ResetPasswordToken has been sent to device' (not 'null or empty, skipping').
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Kusto/Reset Passcode.md

symptom: AAD Connect device writeback fails; some Intune-registered devices cannot sync back to on-premises AD. Synchronization error: 'Error in evaluation of expression' for stkDeviceKey with SHA256/FIPS-related error 'This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms'
rootCause: Prior to .NET Framework 4.6.2, SHA256.Create() defaulted to SHA256Managed which is not FIPS-compliant. The device writeback sync rule 'In from AAD - Device Common' evaluates stkDeviceKey using Thumbprint() which calls SHA256, causing FIPS validation failure on servers with FIPS policy enabled.
solution: Upgrade .NET Framework to 4.6.2 or later on the AAD Connect server. In .NET 4.6.2, SHA256.Create() was changed to use SHA256Cng which is FIPS-compliant.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Device writeback] Azure _ Intune _ write back iss.md

symptom: iOS DEP-enrolled device without user affinity shows compliance state 'Not evaluated' (Unknown) in Intune portal instead of Compliant/Noncompliant. Kusto CalculationQueueLibrary shows 'Skipping userless compliance calculation for devices that are not userless capable' and 'Compliance evaluation complete TaskStatus=Failed'
rootCause: DEP enrollment without user affinity does not complete WorkPlaceJoin (WPJ/AAD registration with user context). Without WPJ, the device cannot be evaluated against user-targeted compliance policies. The compliance engine skips calculation for non-userless-capable device types (DeviceType: 8 = iOS).
solution: This is expected behavior for DEP without user affinity. If compliance evaluation is required, use DEP with user affinity so the device completes WPJ and AAD registration with a user. Alternatively, for shared/kiosk devices, create device-group-targeted compliance policies (not user-targeted).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/Android Compliance policy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Android device reported Noncompliant for encryption despite being encrypted; device returns ENCRYPTION_STATUS_ACTIVE_DEFAULT_KEY (value 4) meaning device encrypted with default key (no startup PIN). Setting a startup PIN does not change the reported status.
rootCause: Android getEncryptionStatus() API returns value 4 (ENCRYPTION_STATUS_ACTIVE_DEFAULT_KEY) when device encrypted without startup PIN. Some devices do not update reported encryption status correctly after PIN set.
solution: (1) Verify USB debugging via OMADM log: InstanceSource ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/DeviceLock/DevicePolicyManager/IsUsbDebuggingAllowed; (2) Check getEncryptionStatus() return value - 4=ACTIVE_DEFAULT_KEY needs startup PIN, 3=ACTIVE is expected; (3) If PIN set but status still 4, collect device model and OMADM logs for escalation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/Android compliance API.md

symptom: After upgrading Huawei device to HarmonyOS VNEXT (5.0+), Intune MAM policy stops working; Company Portal logs show NOT_COMPLIANT with rooted device error even though device is not rooted.
rootCause: HarmonyOS VNEXT removed AOSP compatibility and uses sandbox hiding file paths, triggering Intune root detection false positive. MAM only supports Android AOSP-based devices. Confirmed by ICM-636482650.
solution: Intune MAM does NOT support HarmonyOS VNEXT 5.0+. MAM only works on Android AOSP, OpenHarmony, and older HarmonyOS with AOSP layer. DCR may be filed but not on roadmap.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/Harmony VNEXT (5.0 and above) does not support MAM.md

symptom: Huawei device enrollment in Intune fails with overlay error - another app displaying over Settings blocks interaction.
rootCause: Huawei floating window or Quick Memo creates overlay blocking Android Settings activity, preventing device administrator activation during Intune enrollment.
solution: (1) Disable floating window in Settings; (2) If Quick Memo, open Memo app Settings disable Quick Note; (3) Retry enrollment after closing overlay.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/Huawei Enrollment Issue.md

symptom: On Huawei devices, MAM-protected apps show org removed data or prompt to reinstall Company Portal. Apps lose corporate data unexpectedly.
rootCause: Huawei battery optimization kills Company Portal background process, breaking MAM broker session and triggering data wipe.
solution: Exempt CP and Outlook: (1) Phone Manager > Startup Manager enable auto-start/associated/background; (2) Data Management enable all network access; (3) Settings > Apps > Special Access > Ignore Battery Optimization for CP and Outlook.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/Huawei MAM issue.md

symptom: On OPPO devices, MAM-protected apps (Outlook, Edge, OneDrive) fail to open after app protection policy applied.
rootCause: OPPO app optimization blocks Company Portal and Authenticator background execution, preventing MAM broker auth.
solution: Phone Manager > Privacy Permissions: enable Associated Startup, App Permissions, Auto-start for Outlook/Authenticator/CP/Edge. Restart device.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/OPPO MAM issue.md

symptom: Outlook for Android cannot open after MAM policy applied; Company Portal on China app stores is outdated version 5.0.5155.0; Outlook SDK requires newer CP version.
rootCause: China app stores stopped updating CP due to CAC privacy regulations. Outlook Intune SDK requires newer CP version, causing MAM init failure.
solution: WA1: Download CP from Baidu App Store or MS-supported stores. WA2: Download CP APK from microsoft.com/download (id=49140), deploy as LOB Required via Intune.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Android TSG/China app store CP outdated.md

symptom: Intune PowerShell script needs to re-run but IME only executes scripts once; script shows as succeeded but needs to run again with updated content
rootCause: Intune Management Extension caches script execution results in registry at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IntuneManagementExtension\Policies\{UserGUID}\{ScriptGUID}. Once marked as succeeded, IME will not re-execute.
solution: Reset registry values: set DownloadCount=0, ErrorCode=0, Result and ResultDetails to empty string at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IntuneManagementExtension\Policies\{UserGUID}\{ScriptGUID}. Restart 'Microsoft Intune Management Extension' service. Alternative: use scheduled task wrapper to make script run repeatedly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Intune PowerShell Script deploy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Intune portal shows PowerShell script applied successfully but the script does not produce expected results on device; registry keys not found or script behavior differs from local execution
rootCause: IME runs PowerShell scripts using Windows PowerShell (x86) 32-bit by default. 32-bit process is subject to WOW64 registry redirection - registry calls to redirected keys (e.g., HKLM\SOFTWARE) are mapped to WOW6432Node physical locations.
solution: Test script with Windows PowerShell (x86) locally. If x86 cannot run as expected, it is a 32-bit PowerShell limitation. Check WOW64 registry redirection docs. Consider using 64-bit context or adjusting script to handle redirection.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Intune PowerShell Script deploy troubleshooting.md

symptom: Admin with custom Intune RBAC role cannot edit or create compliance discovery scripts; operations fail with permission error
rootCause: Custom RBAC role missing required permissions. Edit script needs PATCH permission, create script needs POST permission on device compliance policies. Additionally, the device scope group of the custom role assignment must cover the scope of compliance policy assignment.
solution: Assign required permissions in custom role: Edit existing script = PATCH, Create new script = POST, Edit script used by deployed compliance policy = requires scope group coverage. Ensure device scope group of role assignment covers compliance policy assignment scope per MS docs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Intune RBAC _ Permission needed for discovery scri.md

symptom: PKCS certificate deployed via Intune does not renew; modifying validity period in certificate profile and re-pushing does not update the certificate expiry on device
rootCause: PKCS certificates are the same certificate for the same device - Intune re-pushes the identical cert. Unlike SCEP which generates a unique certificate per request, PKCS reuses the same cert so validity period changes have no effect.
solution: For PKCS renewal: must revoke/remove existing cert first then redeploy. To remove: unassign profile or use Remove-certificates action. For auto-renewal needs, consider SCEP instead (each request generates unique cert with fresh validity). Ref: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/certificates-configure and learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/remove-certificates
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Intune renew PKCS certificate.md

symptom: PFX Certificate connector keeps contacting decommissioned old CA server instead of newly configured CA; certificates fail to issue even though Intune profile points to new CA
rootCause: PFX connector caches unprocessed certificate requests in a 'processing' folder. Old requests downloaded before CA change still reference old CA. Connector retries these 10 times before marking failed, masking the real issue.
solution: Delete contents of the PFX connector 'processing' folder to clear backlog of old CA requests. Connector will then download fresh requests pointing to new CA. Ref ICM-418855554.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Known issue - PFX Certificate connector is pointin.md

symptom: LOB app update behavior varies by platform and assignment type; iOS auto-updates silently for Available assignment while Android prompts user if Check apps from external sources is enabled
rootCause: Platform-specific LOB update handling: iOS auto-updates installed Available LOB apps without notice; Android respects Check apps from external sources setting which prompts user for external app installation
solution: Upload LOB app and replace original deployment (verify version reflects update). iOS Available: silent auto-update. iOS Required: auto-update. Android Available/Required: prompt if Check apps from external sources enabled; disable setting for silent update.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/LOB available app update expected behavior.md

symptom: Mac device needs to re-enroll in Intune or IntuneMdmDaemon process is stuck/unresponsive
rootCause: MDM enrollment profile expired or corrupted, or IntuneMdmDaemon process hung
solution: Run 'sudo profiles renew -type enrollment' to re-enroll Mac in Intune. If IntuneMdmDaemon is stuck, kill it first with 'sudo killall IntuneMdmDaemon'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Mac/Mac re-enroll cmd.md

symptom: Windows devices unexpectedly join or auto-enroll to wrong Intune tenant (Global vs 21v) in dual-federation environment with SCCM co-existence; VDI session hosts enroll to incorrect cloud
rootCause: Service Connection Point (SCP) registry keys cloned from master/snapshot VDI images point to the wrong Azure AD tenant. Additionally, SCCM auto-enrollment policy collection rules may not properly exclude devices by naming convention (e.g., CN prefix). In dual-fed environments with both Global and 21v SCCM, the enrollment target depends on SCP configuration and SCCM collection membership.
solution: (1) Check SCP registry keys on affected device to verify they point to correct tenant; (2) For VDI, ensure master image SCP registry is cleared or set correctly before snapshot; (3) Verify SCCM collection membership and exclusion rules (e.g., device name = CN excluded from global auto-enrollment); (4) Use customer's PowerBI dashboard to audit device-to-collection mapping; (5) For dual-fed tenants, confirm which domains are whitelisted to route to 21v vs Global.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/EY - 安永.md

symptom: Network connectivity drops during Windows new hire onboarding after Intune deploys SCEP certificate and WiFi profile; WiFi disconnects when authentication method switches from PEAP to EAP-TLS on the same SSID
rootCause: When Intune pushes SCEP+WiFi profile to newly enrolled device, the WiFi authentication method changes from PEAP (user credential) to EAP-TLS (certificate-based) on the same SSID. The transition causes temporary network disconnection because the device needs to re-authenticate with the new method on the same network.
solution: (1) Check WiFi connection timestamp vs auth method switch timing to confirm this is the cause; (2) Customer-developed PowerShell script workaround (coordinated with networking team) handles the auth transition; (3) Consider using separate SSIDs for PEAP and EAP-TLS to avoid in-place auth switching; (4) If using ClearPass NAC, coordinate with networking team to ensure smooth cert-based auth transition.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/Xiaohongshu - 小红书.md

symptom: Apple Business Manager (ABM) automated device enrollment fails with HTTP 404 error
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/Xiaohongshu - 小红书.md

symptom: macOS FileVault profile is installed via Intune but FileVault is not enabled on the device. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows iOSPlugin: FileVault payload is installed but FileVault is not enabled. Error code -2016341107 (FileVaultNotEnabled) or -2016341108 (PrerequisitesNotSatisfied).
rootCause: FileVault prerequisites are not met on the device, or the FileVault payload was deployed but the user has not yet enabled FileVault encryption.
solution: Ensure device meets prerequisites. Check Kusto DeviceManagementProvider with device ID and look for iOSPlugin messages. Verify FileVault profile is correctly targeted in device channel (not user channel). Properties like PersonalRecoveryKeyHelpMessage and PersonalRecoveryKeyRotationInMonths are only supported in device channel sessions.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Mac/MacOS FileVault troubleshooting.md

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg wrapped to .intunemac) shows Install status in Company Portal even after successful installation. App is usable but status never changes to Re-install. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows app as Not Installed.
rootCause: The .pkg file contains multiple app installers (e.g., Wireshark has 3 sub-packages), and not all install to /Applications or its child folders. Intune only reports successful installation when ALL installed apps are detected, and detection requires install-location to be /Applications or a child folder.
solution: Extract the .pkg file using 'xar -x -f <pkg> -C <output>' and verify each sub-package install-location. Ensure all packages install to /Applications or its child folder. If the .pkg contains utilities installing to /usr/local/bin or /Library/Application Support, consider repackaging or using a custom detection script.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Mac/macOS LOB app installation status not correct.md

symptom: NDES service stops issuing SCEP certificates after installing Windows Server update KB5014702 on the CA server. Event log shows DCOM authentication errors (Event ID 10036): server-side authentication level policy requires RPC_C_AUTHN_LEVEL_PKT_INTEGRITY.
rootCause: KB5014702 raises DCOM authentication level on the CA server (CVE-2021-26414 hardening). The NDES server running an older Windows version without matching patches cannot authenticate at the required level.
solution: Three options: 1) Install KB5014702 on the NDES server as well. 2) Uninstall KB5014702 from the CA server (not recommended). 3) Set registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Ole\AppCompat\RequireIntegrityActivationAuthenticationLevel (DWORD) to 0 on the CA server.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/Known issue_ NDES service stop to working after in.md

symptom: NDES server stops issuing SCEP certificates and returns HTTP 500 error. Connector event log shows certificate validation failure. Certificates were renewed but NDES still fails.
rootCause: The root CA CRL (Certificate Revocation List) has expired. Even though NDES certificates were renewed, the CRL distribution point shows expired CRL, causing certificate chain validation to fail.
solution: 1. Check CRL status using PKIVIEW.msc on the CA jump server. 2. On the root CA, run 'certutil -crl' to renew CRL. 3. Check registry for actual CRL storage path (may differ from default). 4. Copy new CRL and publish: 'certutil -dspublish -f <CA-name>-CA.crl <CA-name>-CA'. 5. Verify CRL is renewed in PKIVIEW.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD joined device fails to receive user-based SCEP certificate. SCEP profile is configured and assigned but certificates are not delivered to hybrid AADJ devices.
rootCause: SCEP certificate profile Key Storage Provider (KSP) is set to Windows Hello for Business. On Hybrid AADJ devices where WHfB is enabled via GPO, SCEP cannot use WHfB as KSP.
solution: Change the SCEP certificate profile KSP setting from 'Windows Hello for Business' to 'Enroll to TPM KSP if present, otherwise Software KSP'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: SCEP certificate enrollment fails. Testing external NDES URL returns BadGateway error. Internal NDES URL works fine.
rootCause: Azure AD Application Proxy is misconfigured. When multiple web application proxies are installed, the external NDES URL redirects to the wrong proxy connector.
solution: Create an Application Proxy group in Azure AD > Application Proxy. Modify the NDES enterprise application to use the correct proxy group with the right connector assignment.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: iOS devices enrolled via ABM show Azure AD Registered as empty or false in Intune device export. Large number of devices (1400+) affected.
rootCause: iOS devices were activated via ABM but users did not sign into Company Portal app after activation. Multiple enrollment attempts during activation created legacy/residual records, preventing proper Azure AD registration.
solution: Adopt Just in Time (JIT) deployment for ABM enrollment to ensure proper Azure AD registration. Note: previously affected devices may not be recoverable and may need to be re-enrolled.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: iOS/iPadOS devices display compliance status as '未评估' (Not Evaluated) in Intune admin portal after ABM enrollment.
rootCause: Same root cause as Azure AD Registered = false issue. Device did not complete expected registration flow after ABM activation — Company Portal was not signed into, so compliance evaluation cannot proceed.
solution: Use Just in Time (JIT) deployment for new ABM enrollments. Already affected devices likely require re-enrollment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: Intune PowerShell scripts using legacy Microsoft Intune application (client) ID (d1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547) stop working or fail authentication after April 2024.
rootCause: Microsoft removed the global Intune PowerShell application (client) ID based authentication method as part of migration to Microsoft Graph SDK-based PowerShell module. The legacy ID is no longer valid.
solution: 1) Create a new app registration in Microsoft Entra admin center. 2) Update all PowerShell scripts to replace the Intune application ID (d1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547) with the new registered application ID. Reference: https://github.com/microsoft/mggraph-intune-samples
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC721851：Update your PowerShell scripts with a Mic.md

symptom: DDM (Declarative Device Management) policy assigned to all users but some devices do not show the policy under device configuration.
rootCause: Enrollment mechanism and prerequisites not fully established before policy deployment. When device registration/readiness (step 1) is not confirmed, policy assignment (step 2) will have inconsistent results.
solution: Verify enrollment mechanism and device readiness prerequisites before broad DDM policy deployment. Do not deploy policies en masse until enrollment and device state are fully confirmed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: Intune NDES/SCEP connector fails to communicate with Intune service. Error: System.NotSupportedException: The ServicePointManager does not support proxies with the xxx scheme. Connector status shows unhealthy.
rootCause: The Unified Intune Certificate Connector only supports proxy in URL format (http:// or https://). If proxy is configured as bare hostname (e.g., app-uat-proxy.bwainet.net) without scheme prefix, the connector throws NotSupportedException.
solution: On NDES server, navigate to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\PFXCertificateConnector\ConnectorUI, open PFXCertificateConnectorUI.exe, edit proxy configuration to URL format: http://"proxy address" or https://"proxy address". Connector status will return to OK after change.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/New NDES connector proxy issue.md

symptom: Windows clients fail to receive SCEP certificate. SCEP profile shows error -2016409340. NDES event log shows 'Failed to verify SCEP request' with ChallengeExpired error, and RPC server unavailable (0x800706ba) Event ID 31.
rootCause: The NDES service account password expired, causing RPC communication failure between NDES and the CA server. SCEP challenge validation also fails because the expired password prevents the connector from communicating with Intune to validate challenges.
solution: Reset the NDES service account password. After password reset, SCEP certificates can be issued and profiles deploy successfully. Note: gMSA (Group Managed Service Account) is NOT supported by the unified Intune cert connector due to a known cert renewal failure issue with no workaround.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/SCEP not applied succeed.md

symptom: SCEP certificates cannot be issued to clients via NDES. Connector event log shows Event ID 2: 'The client and server cannot communicate, because they do not possess a common algorithm' during SCEP verify request.
rootCause: TLS 1.2 is not enabled or properly configured in .NET Framework on the NDES server. The Intune connector requires TLS 1.2 for communication with Intune service endpoints, but the server defaults to older TLS versions.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2 on the NDES server: 1) Set registry keys HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319 and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319 with SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 and SchUseStrongCrypto=1. 2) Enable TLS 1.2 Server and Client under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2. 3) Restart the server.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/Unable to issue certs through SCEP _ The client an.md

symptom: SharePoint site can be accessed via web browser but user cannot download/print/sync with error: 'Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device. To use these actions, use a device that's joined to a domain or marked compliant by Intune'
rootCause: SharePoint admin enabled 'Allow limited, web-only access' for unmanaged devices in SharePoint admin center. This auto-creates 2 Conditional Access policies: one blocks app access from non-hybrid-AADJ/non-Intune-enrolled devices, another enforces app-enforced restrictions on browser sessions preventing download/print/sync from unmanaged devices.
solution: 1. Verify SharePoint admin center > Access control > Unmanaged devices setting. 2. If 'Allow limited, web-only access' is set, two auto-created CA policies exist. 3. To allow download: either enroll device in Intune or Hybrid AAD Join the device to satisfy the CA policy. 4. Alternatively, SharePoint admin can change to 'Allow full access from desktop apps, mobile apps, and the web' if security posture permits.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/SharePoint site can be access via web browser, but.md

symptom: After deploying app as Available type to user group, end user unable to see the app in Company Portal or app takes very long time to appear; Company Portal app list returns 0 results (resultCount=0 in Kusto HttpSubsystem)
rootCause: Effective group membership change has not yet propagated to Intune backend. When user's group membership changes, EffectiveGroupMembershipsUserService takes time to update. Until PolicyAssignmentProvider picks up new effective group, app assignments are not reflected in Company Portal API responses. Even apps assigned to 'All Users' may be affected during membership transition.
solution: 1. Check Kusto HttpSubsystem for Company Portal API calls: filter by deviceId, verify resultCount. 2. Check EffectiveGroupMembershipsUserService_RawData for EffectiveGroupChangeTime. 3. Check PolicyAssignmentProvider for policy assignment count changes. 4. Compare timestamps between group change and Company Portal query. 5. If resultCount=0 after group change, wait for replication (typically minutes, can be hours). 6. For apps assigned to 'All Users', verify the assignment is effective regardless of group membership change.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Troubleshooting Available app deploy to user group (2).md

symptom: Hybrid AADJ Autopilot provisioning delays 3-4 hours; device stuck at ESP Account Setup phase waiting for Azure AD registration
rootCause: The Automatic-Device-Join scheduled task under \Microsoft\Windows\Workplace Join is disabled after domain join. The task should be enabled by default after domain join + GPO apply, but remains disabled for ~2 hours, preventing timely HAADJ registration.
solution: Workaround: Deploy Intune PowerShell script during WhiteGlove to run 'Enable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Workplace Join" -TaskName "Automatic-Device-Join"' to force-enable the task immediately after domain join, speeding up HAADJ registration.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Autopilot provisioning slow HAADJ.md

symptom: Windows Intune enrollment fails; OMA-DM sync does not execute after omadmprc.exe activation; SessionState set to 5 prematurely; error SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION (0x80041313)
rootCause: A corrupted scheduled task trigger registry entry (e.g., Media Center RecordingRestart task) has invalid trigger version byte (0xFF instead of 0x17/0x16/0x15) at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache\Tasks\{TaskGUID}\Triggers. Task Scheduler returns SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION to omadmprc.exe, which interprets it as activation failure and sets SessionState=5 (completed). When omadmclient.exe starts, it sees SessionState=5 and skips SyncML.
solution: Identify the corrupted task trigger registry entry. Fix or delete the invalid Triggers binary value under the offending TaskCache\Tasks\{GUID}. The corrupted task is unrelated to Intune (e.g., Media Center) but blocks OMA-DM session activation globally.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/High level Windows Enrollment.md

symptom: Microsoft Intune Enrollment service principal (AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c) missing from Azure AD; MDM auto-enrollment fails
rootCause: The 'Microsoft Intune Enrollment' enterprise application was accidentally deleted from Azure AD, breaking MDM enrollment flow.
solution: 1) Go to Azure portal > Azure Active Directory > MDM > select 'Microsoft Intune Enrollment' > Delete. 2) Open admin PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD, then run New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c. Verify with Get-AzureADServicePrincipal | Where-Object DisplayName -like '*Intune*'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/How to add back Microsoft Intune enrollment.md

symptom: Intune-managed Windows device cannot receive remote commands or policy updates immediately; device only syncs at default 8-hour check-in interval
rootCause: Windows Push Notification Services (WNS) channel is not working. Common causes: (1) HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\*\Push key missing or channel expired; (2) WNS service not running or unstable; (3) Dependency services disabled — 'Network Connection Broker' (ncbservice) or 'Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant'; (4) Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant disabled prevents device compact ticket generation, causing Event 1116 error 0x80070426.
solution: 1) Check Push reg key exists under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\{EnrollmentID}\Push and verify expiry time is in future. 2) Verify WNS service is running and stable. 3) Ensure dependency services are started: ncbservice and Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant. 4) Check netstat for ESTABLISHED connections to WNS IPs (e.g., 20.198.x.x). 5) Check Event Viewer > Microsoft-Windows-PushNotification-Platform Operational for Event ID 1116. 6) Verify device ticket at HKU\.DEFAULT\Software\Microsoft\IdentityCRL\Immersive\production\Token.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u (2).md

symptom: Users blocked from launching MAM-protected iOS/Android apps after June 2024; app fails to start with MAM policy enforcement error
rootCause: iOS apps using Intune SDK or App Wrapping Tool version older than 17.7.0. Android Company Portal not updated to v5.0.6198.0. Microsoft enforced minimum SDK/wrapper version starting June 1, 2024 (MC775341).
solution: Update iOS LOB apps to Intune SDK/wrapper v17.7.0+ (recommended: SDK 19.2.0). Update Android Company Portal to v5.0.6198.0+. Use Conditional Launch settings: Min SDK version to block apps with SDK < 17.7.0, Min Company Portal version to warn users on CP < 5.0.6198.0.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC775341_ Update to the latest Company Portal for.md

symptom: Newly enrolled Apple (iOS/macOS) devices not appearing in Entra dynamic groups after June 2024; device registration incomplete despite successful MDM enrollment
rootCause: Starting June 2024 (MC777834), Apple device registration was decoupled from enrollment — registration now occurs AFTER enrollment completes. Users must return to Company Portal to finish registration. If user does not return, device is enrolled but not registered, so dynamic groups based on device properties do not populate.
solution: Ensure users return to Company Portal after enrollment to complete registration. Alternatives: (1) Migrate to web-based enrollment with Just-In-Time registration (auto-registers during normal use); (2) Enable Conditional Access policies to force users back to Company Portal; (3) Migrate from dynamic groups to assignment filters (no registration dependency). macOS Company Portal auto-completes registration if not manually closed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC777834：Update for Apple user and device enrollme.md

symptom: Cannot deploy printer drivers to non-administrator users via Intune after KB5005652 security update; Point and Print driver installation blocked
rootCause: KB5005652 (CVE-2021-34481) changed default behavior to restrict printer driver installation to administrators only. Non-admin users can no longer install printer drivers via Point and Print.
solution: Option 1: Deploy PowerShell script via Intune (run as SYSTEM) to set registry HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\PointAndPrint\RestrictDriverInstallationToAdministrators=0 and create scheduled task to run Add-Printer -ConnectionName at logon. Option 2: Run elevated PowerShell as logon user to Add-Printer. Option 3: Use Intune Device Configuration Profile (Printer settings under device restrictions, works for user groups only).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## How To/## Windows/Deploy Printer Driver via Intune.md

symptom: Company Portal (CP) unable to sign in on iOS/Android in 21Vianet (Mooncake) environment; error message: 'This username may be incorrect. Make sure you typed it correctly'
rootCause: User's domain was previously verified in Global Azure AD tenant. When CP attempts authentication, it routes to Global auth page instead of 21v auth page. LogsMiner shows request not being redirected to 21v authentication endpoint.
solution: Temporary workaround: have user first sign in with user@partner.onmschina.cn format, which redirects to correct 21v auth page. Teams and Outlook are not affected because they use office discovery to route to 21v tenant first. If LogsMiner confirms no redirect record, submit IcM (reference: IcM-412709475, IcM-378453964).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Known Issue/CP unable sign in_ This username may be incorrect..md

symptom: Intune Device Restriction policy for password complexity (DeviceLock/MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters) reports 'Error' status on Windows 10 desktop devices; SyncML returns status 405 (command not allowed) with HResult 0x86000011
rootCause: Password complexity value 4 ('Digits, lowercase letters, uppercase letters, and special characters are required') is documented as 'Not supported in desktop' in the Policy CSP. Intune pushes this value via OMA-URI ./Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/DeviceLock/MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters but the Windows MDM client rejects it with 405. This is by design.
solution: Change password complexity setting to a supported value (1, 2, or 3) for Windows desktop devices. Value 4 is only supported on mobile. Use Windows LAPS if customer needs to manage local admin passwords. Kusto: DeviceManagementProvider with EventId 5786 to check policy compliance status. Reference IcMs: 163176114, 160587823.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Known Issue/Policy CSP - Device Lock.md

symptom: Custom OMA-URI device configuration policies may stop working or show unexpected behavior after Intune migrates Windows policies to unified settings platform
rootCause: Microsoft is migrating Windows device configuration policies to unified settings platform. Custom policies (OMA-URI) should only be used when the same setting is NOT available in Settings Catalog.
