symptom: Wired-network profile stopped applying on macOS devices while other configuration profiles work normally.
rootCause: PG-confirmed bug affecting wired-network profile deployment on macOS.
solution: Bug confirmed and fixed by PG (closed Oct 2023). If issue recurs, file ICM referencing this known bug.

rootCause: Service-side issue causing spike in 302 redirect failures on Mooncake Intune infrastructure (CNPXSUCPCN01).
solution: Filed as LSI (Large Scale Incident), MC: IT717941. Service team resolved within 3 days. If similar patterns recur, check for LSI/service health incidents.

symptom: Windows intermediate certificate deployment via Intune failed with error code 0x86000031.
rootCause: Customer-side configuration issue. Resolved by Windows Engineering Escalation team.
solution: Engage Windows EE for investigation. Error 0x86000031 in certificate deployment is typically customer-side. Verify certificate chain, template settings, and NDES/SCEP configuration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## ICM Process/Intune Work-Item reference/Archieve-Intune IcM list.md

symptom: Intune compliance policies and configuration profiles take effect on users who do not have an Intune license assigned.
rootCause: By design. Intune policies can be applied to devices regardless of user license status. License enforcement is at enrollment level, not policy level.
solution: This is expected behavior (by design). Intune license is required for enrollment, but policies deployed to device groups can affect any enrolled device. Communicate to customer that this is by design.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## ICM Process/Intune Work-Item reference/Archieve-Intune IcM list.md

symptom: Android LOB app update prompts user to install instead of auto-updating when assignment type is Available; user sees installation confirmation dialog each time an app update is pushed
rootCause: When 'Check apps from external sources' (Verify apps over USB / Play Protect external app check) is enabled on the Android device, the OS treats the updated APK as an external source installation and prompts user confirmation. iOS LOB apps auto-update silently for both Available and Required assignments.
solution: For silent auto-update on Android: disable 'Check apps from external sources' setting on the device, or change assignment to Required. Inform customer iOS LOB apps auto-update without notice regardless of assignment type. Behavior matrix: iOS Available=auto-update, iOS Required=auto-update, Android Available=prompt if external check enabled, Android Required=prompt if external check enabled.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/LOB available app update expected behavior.md

symptom: Browsing NDES URL (https://<server>/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll) shows certificate warning/insecure connection; browser displays certificate error when accessing NDES service page
rootCause: IIS on the NDES server does not have a valid SSL certificate bound to the HTTPS binding. After installing the NDES role, the default IIS site may not have a trusted certificate configured for SSL.
solution: Configure IIS SSL binding with a valid certificate: 1) Obtain trusted server certificate (from DigiCert or internal CA); 2) IIS Manager > Sites > Default Web Site > Bindings; 3) Edit HTTPS binding and select valid cert; 4) Restart IIS; 5) Verify browsing shows 403 (expected) without cert warning. If using internal CA, ensure CA root cert is trusted by devices.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Lab - NDES/2. NDES.md

symptom: Intune Certificate Connector installation or configuration fails on NDES server; connector setup wizard reports errors about missing prerequisites or IIS features
rootCause: NDES server is missing required IIS role features that are prerequisites for the Certificate Connector. The connector requires specific IIS components (ASP.NET, ISAPI Extensions, Request Filtering) that may not be installed by default with the NDES role.
solution: Install all required IIS prerequisites per Microsoft docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/certificate-connector-prerequisites#general-prerequisites. Re-run installer after installing missing features. Connector exe: C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\PFXCertificateConnector\ConnectorUI\PFXCertificateConnectorUI.exe. Ensure domain account has local admin rights.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Lab - NDES/3. Connector -scep.md

symptom: NDES GetCACaps endpoint (mscep.dll?operation=GetCACaps) stops returning valid CA operations after previously working; SCEP certificate requests fail
rootCause: IIS certificate binding was changed or lost after NDES reconfiguration. HTTPS binding on Default Web Site no longer points to a valid certificate. Can happen after certificate renewal, server restart, or configuration changes.
solution: 1) IIS Manager > Sites > Default Web Site > Bindings; 2) Verify HTTPS binding has valid cert; 3) Re-bind correct certificate if needed; 4) iisreset; 5) Test browsing GetCACaps - should return CA operations list; 6) Verify NDES registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP points to correct template.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Lab - NDES/3. Connector -scep.md

symptom: SCEP certificate request fails on device with error 0x86000022. CspCreateInstance of Node fails during SCEP enrollment.
rootCause: Error 0x86000022 indicates SCEP CSP instance creation failure on the device side. Typically caused by missing or corrupt certificate prerequisites, or NDES connectivity issue from device.
solution: Check device-side SCEP logs. Verify NDES URL is reachable from device. Ensure Trusted Root CA certificate profile is deployed before SCEP profile. Review IIS logs on NDES server for corresponding request. If error persists, collect device diagnostic logs and check Event Viewer > Application and Service Logs > Microsoft > Intune > Certificate Connectors on NDES server.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Lab - NDES/Device log.md

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg) fails to install via Intune. App shows NotInstalled in Kusto. Running .pkg locally works fine. MDM Console log shows 'failed Error Domain=NSCocoaErrorDomain Code=4 The file Distribution does not exist'.
rootCause: The .pkg file was not built as a distribution archive using productbuild. Apple MDM requires packages to be proper distribution archives with a signed certificate (Developer ID Installer). A component package alone (built only with pkgbuild) is not sufficient for MDM distribution.
solution: 1. Build component package: pkgbuild --root <payload> --scripts <scripts> --version <version> --identifier <id> ./<name>_component.pkg. 2. Create distribution archive: productbuild --package <name>_component.pkg <name>.pkg. 3. Sign with Developer ID Installer certificate. Verify signature: pkgutil --check-signature <name>.pkg (leaf cert must show 'Developer ID Installer'). 4. Wrap with IntuneAppUtil to create .intunemac. 5. If download hangs, restart downloadd process via Activity Monitor or reboot Mac.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MacOS TSG/MacOS App deployment - pkg &dmg/Intune_ Troubleshooting guidelines for installing.md

symptom: macOS LOB app update deployed via Intune does not apply on device. Device still shows old version. App was repackaged but Intune does not detect the new version.
rootCause: The updated .pkg and .intunemac file do not have incremented BuildNumber and VersionNumber compared to the base app. Intune uses these version fields from Detection.xml inside the .intunemac package for update detection.
solution: 1. Increment <version> parameter in pkgbuild when creating the updated component package. 2. Rebuild distribution archive with productbuild. 3. Re-wrap with IntuneAppUtil. 4. Verify detection parameters: ./IntuneAppUtil -r <filename.intunemac> -v — check that BuildNumber and VersionNumber are higher than the currently deployed version. 5. Both base app BundleId and version fields must match for in-place update.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MacOS TSG/MacOS App deployment - pkg &dmg/Intune_ Troubleshooting guidelines for installing.md

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg) with multiple sub-components installs successfully but Company Portal shows 'Install' instead of 'Re-Install'. Kusto reports NotInstalled. App is functional on device.
rootCause: The .pkg file contains multiple component installers (e.g., Wireshark has 3 sub-packages). Some components install to non-/Applications paths (e.g., /Library/Application Support, /usr/local/bin). Intune only reports 'Installed' when ALL components are detected, and detection requires install-location to be /Applications or its child folder.
solution: 1. Extract the pkg to inspect: xar -x -f <pkg> -C <output>. 2. Check install-location for each component in PackageInfo. 3. If sub-components install outside /Applications, repackage so detection works — either consolidate all into /Applications path or deploy each distribution archive separately. 4. Reference: If a .pkg contains multiple apps/installers, Intune only reports installed when ALL are detected.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MacOS TSG/MacOS App deployment - pkg &dmg/Mac - multiple app install.md

symptom: macOS FileVault is enabled by the user before Intune enrollment or before FileVault policy is deployed. Intune reports error: FileVault is enabled but not via a FileVault payload. Report an error as this prevents us from backing up a personal recovery key. Error code -2016341106 (FileVaultEnabledByUser).
rootCause: FileVault was already enabled by the end user (not via MDM payload). Intune cannot manage the recovery key escrow because it did not initiate the encryption. The FileVault profile is present but Intune has no control over the existing recovery key.
solution: 1. Deploy a FileVault policy to the device from Intune (it will not decrypt/re-encrypt, just enables Intune to assume management). 2. Direct the device user to upload their existing personal recovery key to Intune via Company Portal or the prompted workflow. 3. Once the key is validated, Intune assumes management and generates a new recovery key. 4. Communicate to users via IT channels since Intune does not auto-alert users to upload their key.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MacOS TSG/MacOS FileVault troubleshooting.md

symptom: macOS FileVault policy deployed via Intune shows FileVault payload is installed but FileVault is not enabled. Kusto logs show error code -2016341107 (FileVaultNotEnabled). CommandResult did not contain a FileVault personal recovery key.
rootCause: The FileVault MDM profile was successfully installed on the device, but the actual FileVault disk encryption has not been turned on yet. This typically happens when the device requires a user logout/restart to trigger the FileVault enablement prompt, or the user dismissed the prompt.
solution: 1. Ensure the user logs out and logs back in (or restarts) after the FileVault profile is installed - macOS requires a logout to present the FileVault enablement dialog. 2. Verify prerequisites are met (device must have a SecureToken-enabled user). 3. Check Kusto with iOSPlugin filter for FileVault-related messages to confirm profile installation status. 4. If the user never sees the prompt, check if the profile is stuck - review Console logs for sysextd/mdmclient entries.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MacOS TSG/MacOS FileVault troubleshooting.md

symptom: Windows 10 Kiosk mode with 'Local User Account' logon type fails with error 'No mapping between account names and security IDs was done'. The specified kiosk account does not exist on device.
rootCause: When 'Local User Account' is selected as kiosk logon type, Windows tries to map to an already existing local account on the device. If the account does not exist, the mapping fails. Unlike previous behavior, the account is no longer auto-created when using Local User Account type.
solution: Use 'Auto-logon' as the Logon type instead of 'Local User Account'. Auto-logon creates a local 'Kiosk' account automatically on the device and logs in after every restart. All kiosk policies are applied correctly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/118112726006109 - kiosk mode [REOPEN]AssignAccesse.md

symptom: Windows 10 device fails to auto-enroll in Intune via GPO 'Enable automatic MDM enrollment using default Azure AD credential'. Scheduled Task for MDM enrollment is not created. GPResult shows MDM Policy extension skipped.
rootCause: Local GPO setting 'Turn off Local Group Policy Objects processing' was enabled, which blocks all local GPO processing including the MDM Policy extension. GPSVC log shows 'ReadGPOList count = 0' and 'Extension MDM Policy skipped because both deleted and changed GPO lists are empty'. Additionally, MDM authority was not set to Intune.
solution: 1) Deploy 'Enable automatic MDM enrollment using default Azure AD credential' via Domain GPO instead of Local GPO to bypass the local GPO blocking. 2) Verify MDM authority is set to Intune (not Unknown). 3) After fixing both, confirm Task Schedule is created and device enrolls successfully.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/20870760 - GPO not create Task Scheduler with Enab.md

symptom: Windows trusted certificate (Sub CA) deployment via Intune fails with error 0x86000031 (-2016281112). Certificate shows Error compliance state in portal.
rootCause: Same certificate is simultaneously targeted to both User group and Computer certificate store. Windows enforces: (1) Deny if device-wide resource profile tries to provision a profile already provisioned as user-targeted, and vice-versa. Registry at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\EnterpriseResourceManager\Tracked shows the certificate is already a device-wide resource (./device/Vendor/MSFT/RootCATrustedCertificates/CA/...), so user-targeted deployment is denied.
solution: 1) Identify conflicting policies: check if the same certificate thumbprint is deployed via both user-targeted and device-targeted profiles. 2) Remove one of the conflicting policies so certificate is only pushed to either User or Computer certificate store, not both. 3) Alternatively, clean up stale registry entries under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\EnterpriseResourceManager\Tracked\{enrollment-guid}\device\default to remove the conflict.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2308110030002206 _ Windows Sub CA install failed,.md

symptom: iOS VPP app install fails with VPP error code 9632: Too many recent calls to manage licenses with identical requests.
rootCause: Too many devices for one user or too many Apple IDs making requests from the same company, causing Apple to throttle license management calls.
solution: Switch to device-based VPP licensing instead of user-based licensing to avoid per-user Apple ID conflicts and throttling.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: iOS VPP app install fails with message VPP user licensing not supported for userless enrollment. Device enrolled without user affinity cannot use VPP user licensing.
rootCause: VPP user licensing requires user affinity on the device. Devices enrolled via DEP/Apple Configurator without user affinity can only use device-based VPP licensing.
solution: Change VPP app assignment to device-based licensing. If app is targeted to device groups, ensure license type is set to Device not User. Escalate to PG if device licensing is already configured but still fails.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: All iOS VPP app installs fail for a given user with LicenseNotFound. Intune says license exists but Apple says license not found.
rootCause: The user updated their Apple ID on one of their devices. Apple system records the original Apple ID (used when user first accepted VPP agreement), and the new Apple ID does not match, causing LicenseNotFound.
solution: 1) Log back in with the original Apple ID used when the user first accepted Apple VPP agreement. 2) Move to device-based licensing. 3) Create a brand new Intune user and associate a brand new Apple ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: iOS VPP app install fails with message Your organization has depleted its licenses for this app. VppDeviceLicenseAssignmentFailedEvent seen in Kusto.
rootCause: User licensing allows installation on 5-10 devices per user. All available VPP user licenses for this app have been consumed across the user devices.
solution: Either purchase more VPP licenses, switch to device-based licensing, or temporarily revoke app deployment from the user to reclaim licenses, then redeploy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: Windows HAADJ device shows Intune sync error 0x80190190 for all logon users. dsregcmd shows valid PRT for some users but sync still fails. Multiple users log on to the same device.
rootCause: Non-primary logon users on the device do not have Intune licenses assigned. When multiple users log on to the same Intune-managed Windows device, all users must have valid Intune licenses for sync to succeed. The sync error 0x80190190 occurs specifically because the unlicensed user sync request is rejected.
solution: Assign Intune licenses to all users who log on to the device, then trigger sync again. Use Kusto to identify the primary user vs logon users and verify license assignment in Entra ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2312250050000110 _ Intune Sync error 0x80190190.md

symptom: iOS VPP app is not ready for install. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows iOSPlugin[IsVppAppReadyForInstall]: iOS VPP app is not ready for install, since user with vppApplicationTokenId 00000000 yet to accept VPP invite.
rootCause: User has not accepted the VPP invite. If user did not receive invite, Apple App Store may be blocked on the device (use Hide App option instead of blocking the store entirely).
solution: 1. Verify user has accepted VPP invite. 2. If invite was not received, check if Apple App Store is blocked - use Hide App option rather than block. 3. Resend VPP invite if needed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: VPP apps are not automatically updating on iOS devices even though the tenant has auto-update setting enabled.
rootCause: VPP auto-update is not supported for devices with iOS version below 11.3. Apple does not process auto-update requests for older OS versions.
solution: Update the iOS device to 11.3 or later to enable VPP app auto-update. For devices that cannot be updated, manually update apps through the Company Portal or reassign the app.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2308160010000983 _ Troubleshooting iOS VPP app ins.md

symptom: Win32 app installation fails with error code -2147024894 (0x80070002) and Failed to create installer process. Error code = 2 in IME log. Install command shows specific filename but CreateProcess fails.
rootCause: The install command filename specified in the Intune Win32 app configuration does not match the actual filename inside the .intunewin package. Error code 2 = FILE_NOT_FOUND, meaning the installer executable cannot be found in the IMECache directory.
solution: 1. Rename .intunewin to .zip and inspect Detection.xml for the actual setup filename. 2. Or use IntuneWinAppUtilDecoder to extract the original installer. 3. Compare extracted filename with the install command in Intune portal. 4. Update the install command to match the actual filename.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2309070060002493 _ Intune application install fail.md

symptom: macOS update policy with Install Later action fails for major OS upgrades with MCMDMErrorDomain ErrorCode 12008: Unsupported InstallAction for majorOS update. ScheduleOSUpdate command returns Error status.
rootCause: Apple limitation: Install Later action is not supported for major OS updates. When a major OS upgrade is available, update policies always attempt the latest update first, causing Install Later to fail even for minor updates. After macOS 12.5+, Intune only targets the latest update.
solution: 1. Do not configure Install Later when a major OS upgrade is available. 2. Use Install ASAP or schedule maintenance window for major upgrades. 3. Doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/software-updates-macos.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2311210040004529 _ MacOS update.md

symptom: Android LOB app stuck in ERROR_APP_INSTALL_CANCELLED state. App download initiates but installation gets cancelled repeatedly. OMADMLog shows app state cycling between ERROR_APP_INSTALL_CANCELLED.
rootCause: In China mainland, Google Play services are blocked by firewall. Android LOB app download through Intune DownloadService hangs because device cannot complete download handshake without Google Play services.
solution: 1. During app download, manually switch to Company Portal Settings and click Sync button to trigger download. 2. This manual sync forces re-initiation through DownloadService. 3. Consider alternative app distribution for China deployments. 4. ICM: 448090152.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2311290010002679 _ Android LOB application install.md

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg) re-packaged by third-party tool (e.g., sudre) installs successfully but Intune portal shows failed/not installed. MDM logs: Did not find bundle path URL for app. App gets re-installed repeatedly.
rootCause: Re-packaged .pkg apps have empty install-location key in bundle info (pkgutil --pkg-info-plist shows empty string). Intune Sidecar AppDetection.swift uses pkgutil to resolve app path from install-location. Without valid value, detection cannot find the installed app.
solution: 1. Run pkgutil --pkg-info-plist <bundleID> to verify install-location is populated (should be Applications). 2. Re-package the .pkg with correct install-location in PackageInfo. 3. Compare with working apps to confirm format.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2312050030004587 _ The installation status of mana.md

symptom: iOS/macOS device hardware page in Intune portal shows unknown for some fields and inconsistent values for Supervised/Encrypted status. ADE enrolled device should show Yes but shows incorrect value.
rootCause: Platform bug (ICM 430321468): When scheduled inventory refresh was skipped because device was offline, Intune service called wrong API and failed to get history inventory data, displaying unknown or stale values.
solution: 1. Wait for platform fix (ICM 430321468). 2. When device comes online, full inventory refresh triggers automatically. 3. Perform Check Status in Company Portal to force immediate full inventory report.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2312120040002424 _ 2308140040000805_ Device Check-.md

symptom: DEP token shows warning and sync between Intune and Apple ABM fails. Kusto IntuneEvent shows SessionTokenFailure with 'Malformed or Invalid Token used for Authenticating with Apple' and SyncMultiTokenDevicesFailureEvent.
rootCause: Customer downloaded the DEP token from ABM portal which caused the existing token uploaded to Intune to become invalid. Downloading a new DEP token from ABM invalidates any previously downloaded token. Also, having multiple DEP tokens mapped to same ABM server (1:multiple) causes duplicated tokens in Intune to stop working.
solution: 1. Renew the DEP token: download a new token from ABM and re-upload to Intune. 2. Ensure DEP tokens are 1:1 matching between ABM and Intune - do not create multiple tokens for the same MDM server. 3. Check Kusto: IntuneEvent | where * contains 'StatelessMTAppleDepSyncService' | where AccountId == '<tenantId>' to verify sync status.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2401150040000806 _ DEP token show warning, no sync.md

symptom: VPP app with device licensing keeps prompting iOS users to click Upgrade even though the app is already updated. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows state 'PromptingForUpdate' with externalVersionIdentifier mismatch - installed version shows new ID but Expected version reverts to old ID.
rootCause: After a new app version is released in Apple App Store, Intune initially detects the correct new externalVersionIdentifier. However, on subsequent device check-ins, the Expected version reverts to the old externalVersionIdentifier, causing Intune to send unnecessary PromptingForUpdate commands to the iOS device. This appears to be a transient backend version caching issue in Intune service.
solution: 1. Issue is typically transient and resolves after 1-2 weeks. 2. Verify via Apple lookup API: https://uclient-api.itunes.apple.com/WebObjects/MZStorePlatform.woa/wa/lookup?version=2&p=mdm-lockup&caller=MDM&cc=cn&l=en-us&id={appId}. 3. Check Kusto DeviceManagementProvider for EventId 5748 to see installed vs expected externalVersionIdentifier. 4. If persistent, escalate to PG with evidence of version mismatch.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2401250040002891 _ VPP App with device licensing p.md

symptom: Intune policy RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess deployed successfully (registry and policy manager confirmed correct) but users can still write data to USB drives. MDM diagnostic shows policy status successful and registry value is set correctly.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Storage\HotplugSecureOpen exists on the device, set by a separate Intune Audit policy. When HotplugSecureOpen exists, the Windows file system skips applying the volume security descriptor (which includes the deny-write rule), so the USB block policy has no effect even though registry values are correct.
solution: 1. Check if HotplugSecureOpen registry key exists: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Storage\HotplugSecureOpen. 2. Remove the registry key and restart WPD services to re-enable USB write blocking. 3. Identify and remove the conflicting Audit policy that creates HotplugSecureOpen. 4. Can deploy PowerShell script via Intune to remove the key: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Storage' -Name 'HotplugSecureOpen'. 5. Reference: Storage Policy CSP - ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Storage/RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2402020030000730 _ RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess no.md

symptom: Mac enrollment fails with HTTP 404 error when installing management profile. MDM_Authenticate PUT request gets 302 redirect to /EnrollmentServer/CertificateFallback.aspx which returns 404. Working device gets 200 response directly. Log shows 'No matching identities found for protection space' on failing device.
rootCause: PG confirmed this was a Node issue in the Mooncake (21Vianet) environment. The specific enrollment service node handling the CertificateFallback.aspx request was malfunctioning, causing 404 responses for certain devices while others (routed to different nodes) worked normally.
solution: 1. Collect macOS MDM client logs (mdmclient) to compare working vs non-working enrollment. 2. Check ScrubbedIISLogs for 302/404 patterns on /DeviceGatewayProxy/ioscheckinhandler.ashx and /EnrollmentServer/CertificateFallback.aspx endpoints. 3. If consistent 404 on CertificateFallback.aspx, escalate to PG with IcM - likely a node-level issue. 4. Verify enrollment service health via IOSEnrollmentService and HttpSubsystem Kusto tables.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2402180060000131 _ Unable to complete Mac Enrollme.md

symptom: SCEP certificate not delivered to devices. NDES URL returns HTTP 500 error. Event log shows RA certificate related errors on NDES server. RA certificates cannot be found in NDES server certificate store.
rootCause: Multiple compounding issues: (1) RA certificates were missing from NDES server. (2) NDES role reinstallation required Domain Admin account. (3) IIS certificate did not match NDES server name. (4) NDES service account was not in 'Log on as service' security policy. (5) SCEP Profile URL in Intune was misconfigured.
solution: 1. Check RA certificates in NDES server cert store - if missing, reinstall NDES Role using Domain Admin account. 2. Verify IIS certificate matches NDES server FQDN. 3. Add NDES service account to 'Log on as service' local security policy. 4. Verify SCEP Profile URL format: https://<NDES-FQDN>/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll. 5. Check IIS logs for /certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll/pkiclient.exe requests to confirm traffic is reaching NDES. 6. If GPO changes were made recently, review security group membership impact on NDES configuration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2403210040002578 _ Unable to receive SCEP certific.md

symptom: Win32 app toast notification still showing during supersedence scenario even though 'hide all notifications' is configured on the new version app. Specifically, the uninstall notification of the old version app appears.
rootCause: When a Win32 app supersedes an old version, IME uninstalls the old version and installs the new one. The toast notification setting is per-app assignment. If the old version app's assignment is deleted or does not have 'hide all notifications' configured, the uninstall toast notification will appear because by default toast notifications are enabled (ToastState not set to 2).
solution: 1. Keep the old version app's assignment in Intune (do not delete it). 2. Set the old version app assignment to also use 'hide all notifications'. 3. Verify in IME log: look for 'Skip PopToastNotification since policy is to suppress all notifications' with ToastState: 2. 4. If ToastState is not 2 for uninstall messages, the old app assignment needs notification suppression configured.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2407030030008557 - Hide Toast notification not wor.md

symptom: Intune device diagnostics log collection from portal fails intermittently or takes approximately 8 hours to complete. Some attempts succeed quickly (8 min) while others on the same device take hours. createDeviceLogCollectionRequest returns 200 but WindowsLogCollectionCompleted takes very long.
rootCause: Intermittent issue with the log collection upload to logmanagerservice endpoint. Backend shows createDeviceLogCollectionRequest succeeds (HTTP 200) and WindowsLogCollectionCompleted eventually reports success with ErrorCode: 0, but the roundtrip time varies significantly. Could not reproduce in test environment where collection completes within 20 minutes consistently.
solution: 1. Check HttpSubsystem for logcollect requests to see timing: HttpSubsystem | where * contains '<deviceId>' | where * contains 'logcollect'. 2. Check DeviceManagementProvider for WindowsLogCollectionCompleted events. 3. If collection is slow but backend shows success, the bottleneck may be device-side upload to logmanagerservice endpoint. 4. Retry collection - issue is intermittent. 5. If persistent, escalate to PG with Kusto evidence showing timing discrepancy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Mooncake Case Study\2401020030001644 _ Intune managed devices are unab.md

symptom: Collecting diagnostic logs from Intune portal fails or takes ~8 hours. Issue happens on almost all customer devices. WNS push notification (Event ID 42/2415) appears but device is not woken up for log collection.
rootCause: Group Policy (GPO) has cloud notifications disabled. Registry key NoCloudApplicationNotification under SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications is set to 1, which blocks WNS from waking up the MDM agent. Flow: Admin requests diagnostics -> Intune sends WNS wakeup -> WNS blocked by GPO -> device never wakes up.
solution: 1. Check GPO: Computer Configuration > Start Menu and Taskbar > Notifications > Turn off notifications network usage must be Disabled or Not Configured. 2. Ensure registry NoCloudApplicationNotification = 0. 3. Enable Cloud Notifications/WindowsMDMPush in GPO.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2401020030001644 _ Intune managed devices are unab.md

symptom: macOS enrollment fails with HTTP 404 error during management profile installation. Device log shows 302 redirect to CertificateFallback.aspx then 404. mdmclient reports No matching identities found for protection space during MDM_Authenticate.
rootCause: Intune service Node issue (PG confirmed via ICM 388482221/473790930). During MDM_Authenticate step, the device fails to provide TLS client identity, gets redirected to CertificateFallback.aspx which returns 404.
solution: 1. PG confirmed Node issue - escalate via ICM. 2. Verify enrollment service works: Kusto IOSEnrollmentService by serial number. 3. Check HttpSubsystem for the enrollment activity. 4. Collect device mdmclient logs. ICM: 388482221, 473790930.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2402180060000131 _ Unable to complete Mac Enrollme.md

symptom: Win32 app supersedence scenario: hide toast notification setting works for new app install but uninstall notification for old app still shows. After removing old version app assignment, toast notifications reappear.
rootCause: When old version app assignment is deleted, the hide notification (ToastState: 2) setting is lost. IME uses default toast behavior (show notifications) for uninstall operations of the superseded app.
solution: 1. Keep old version app assignment active and set it to Hide all toast notifications as well. 2. Do not delete old version app assignment if you want notifications hidden during supersedence uninstall. 3. Verify in IME logs: look for Skip PopToastNotification with ToastState: 2.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2407030030008557 - Hide Toast notification not wor.md

symptom: Intune policy RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess deployed successfully (registry value correct), but USB write access is NOT blocked. Users can still write data to USB drives.
rootCause: Conflict with Audit removable storage policy. The Audit policy sets registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Storage\HotplugSecureOpen. When HotplugSecureOpen exists, the file system skips applying volume security descriptor, so block does not take effect. This is a timing issue when both policies are deployed simultaneously.
solution: Remove HotplugSecureOpen registry key and restart WPD services. Or remove the Audit removable storage policy first, wait for cleanup (may take 1 hour or require reboot), then apply RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess. Windows PG is aware of the bug.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2402020030000730 _ RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess no.md

symptom: SCEP certificates not delivered to devices despite NDES server and connector working. No /certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll/pkiclient.exe requests in IIS logs.
rootCause: SCEP profile URL incorrectly configured in Intune. Devices never sent certificate requests to the NDES server.
solution: Verify and correct SCEP profile URL in Intune to https://<NDES-FQDN>/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll. After updating, verify IIS logs show incoming pkiclient.exe requests.

symptom: Windows devices stuck with managed name format (username_platform_timestamp) instead of actual device name after Intune enrollment. Device status shows unknown and incompliant. Most customer devices affected.
rootCause: Enrollment did not complete fully. Device stayed in initial enrollment state where only managed name is assigned before device check-in updates the device name.
solution: Collect MDM diagnostic logs (winevt, dsregcmd output). Check registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments for enrollment state. Verify enrollment completed by checking device check-in status in Kusto.

symptom: Win32 app toast notification for supersedence (uninstall of old version) still shows on Windows device even though Hide all toast notifications is configured in the new app assignment.
rootCause: Old app assignment controls the uninstall toast notification. If old app assignment does not have Hide all toast notifications enabled, the uninstall toast still appears. IME logs show Skip PopToastNotification with ToastState: 2 for new app but old app uninstall triggers separate notification.
solution: Keep the old (superseded) app assignment in Intune and set it to Hide all toast notifications as well. Both new and old app assignments must have notification suppression enabled. Do NOT delete old app assignment if you want to suppress all notifications during supersedence.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2407030030008557 - Hide Toast notification not wor.md

symptom: Security Baseline last report time in Intune portal does not update in sync with device last check-in time; customer expects them to match.
rootCause: By design, Security Baseline report data is copied every 24 hours. If there are no data changes, the delta change report is only generated every 7 days.
solution: This is expected behavior. Inform customer: (1) Report data refreshes every 24 hours; (2) If no changes detected, delta report refreshes every 7 days; (3) Last check-in time reflects MDM sync, not report data refresh.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2408130030000438 _ Security Baseline last Report t.md

symptom: Windows Update Ring configured with Feature update delay of 365 days, expected client to receive Windows 11 22H2, but client receives Windows 11 23H2 instead.
rootCause: The 'Enable Windows 11 latest' toggle was enabled in the update ring, causing the device to scan for the latest feature update. Even after turning it off, stale Windows Update scan cache caused 23H2 to continue being offered.
solution: 1) Disable 'Enable Windows 11 latest' toggle in the update ring; 2) Verify all WU registry keys (HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate, etc.) to ensure only Intune update ring policy is active; 3) Clear WU scan cache: net stop wuauserv, ren C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution SoftwareDistribution.old, net start wuauserv; 4) Re-scan Windows Update.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2409020030005862 _ Windows Update ring not working.md

symptom: Intune auto-enrollment via GPO fails on some VMs because Group Policy is not delivered in time after VM start.
rootCause: After VM boot, GPO was not delivered to the VM automatically. Without GPO, the MDM auto-enrollment registry keys and scheduled task are not created.
solution: Run gpupdate /force to force GPO update, then verify with gpresult /r that the MDM auto-enrollment policy is applied. Once GPO is delivered, VM enrolls to Intune successfully.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2507150040000311 Intune Enroll failed.md

symptom: Intune enrollment fails for some VMs/users - devices cannot enroll.
rootCause: The users assigned to those VMs do not have Intune licenses assigned.
solution: Assign Intune license to the affected users. After license assignment, VMs associated with those users enroll successfully.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2507150040000311 Intune Enroll failed.md

symptom: Intune auto-enrollment via GPO fails: scheduled task not created, dmenrollengine.dll reads EnrollmentState=2 from registry and does not trigger enrollment.
rootCause: SCCM (co-management) legacy enrollment info remains in registry at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\{GUID}. EnrollmentState=2 means enrolled via profile with mismatch, a leftover from SCCM. This prevents GPO-triggered auto-enrollment from creating the scheduled task.
solution: 1) Remove the registry key under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments where EnrollmentState=2 (PowerShell or batch script); 2) For batch cleanup across multiple machines, deploy script via GPO Startup Scripts (place .bat in DC sysvol share); 3) Optionally run Wipe-ConfigMgrAgent.ps1 to fully clean SCCM artifacts; 4) After cleanup, gpupdate /force triggers enrollment task creation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2507150040000311 Intune Enroll failed.md

symptom: NDES cannot process SCEP certificate requests; no SCEP requests appear in event logs; NDES external and internal service URLs return gateway timeout.
rootCause: IIS related service on the NDES server was stopped.
solution: Restart the IIS related services on the NDES server. Verify NDES service URLs are accessible after restart.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2509080040000463 _ XHS NDES structure.md

symptom: Intune SCEP certificate issuance fails with error: The revocation function was unable to check revocation because the revocation server was offline.
rootCause: Published Root CA CRL (.crl file) was expired. The CRL distribution point (CDP) on the web server had Require SSL enabled, preventing proper CRL access.
solution: 1) Untick Require SSL on the IIS site hosting the CRL distribution point; 2) Manually import/publish the updated .crl file to the CDP location; 3) Verify with certutil -verify -urlfetch cert.cer to check CRL chain validity.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2509080040000463 _ XHS NDES structure.md

symptom: Intune Configuration Policy not applied immediately during Autopilot v2 provisioning; filter rule (deviceTrustType eq AAD join) evaluates as Unknown for up to 48 hours after device registration.
rootCause: Entra ID does not support Strong Consistency on national clouds (Mooncake/FairFax). After device creation in Entra, Intune sync gets NotFound due to replication delay, falling back to daily message retry with up to 24-hour delay for deviceTrustType to populate.
solution: 1) Hotfix deployed 2025-10-30/31 to fix the delay; 2) Workaround before hotfix: use user-based groups with filter mode instead of device-based static groups; 3) Verify via Kusto IntuneEvent query filtering by device ID and devicetrusttype to confirm replication delay.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2509190030003863 _ Configuration Policy apply dela.md

symptom: Chrome app push installation via Company Portal fails with error app or device not found; IME log shows UnauthorizedAccessException when unzipping Win32App in C:\WINDOWS\IMECache, error code -2016214937.
rootCause: ChromeSetup.exe is an online stub installer that downloads Chrome from Google servers during installation. In restricted network environments (whitelist-based proxy), the stub installer cannot reach Google download servers. Additionally, IMECache folder permission issues cause UnauthorizedAccessException during unzip.
solution: 1) Use the Chrome MSI full offline installer instead of the online ChromeSetup.exe stub; 2) Package the MSI as a Win32 app (.intunewin) and redeploy; 3) For IMECache access issues, check if C:\WINDOWS\IMECache folder has correct SYSTEM permissions; 4) Company Portal logs location: C:\Users\{user}\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.CompanyPortal_8wekyb3d8bbwe\LocalState (or use Help & Support > Send Logs).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2510150040001133 _ Chrome推送安装失败.md

symptom: Intune always reports Company Portal (WinCP) LOB app deployment as Failure on Windows clients. Detection finds installed version mismatch. Error 0x86000022 returned by device during install attempt. Kusto shows versioned detection unsuccessful for Windows8App.
rootCause: Store-signed apps like Company Portal receive automatic updates from Windows Update service, regardless of LOB deployment method. When CP auto-updates (e.g., from 11.2.1495.0 to 11.2.1672.0), Intune detection fails because it checks for the original deployed version which no longer matches. This occurs even without winget support in 21v.
solution: PG confirmed: this is expected behavior for store-signed LOB apps. 1) Accept the failure status as cosmetic. 2) Use winget to download CP: winget download company portal --source msstore. 3) Do NOT use Download Center link. 4) PG is exploring alternative deployment methods.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2510230040002401 _ Intune always report Company Po.md

symptom: After Teams Win32 deployment succeeds via Autopilot on Windows 24H2, end users cannot find Teams app. Intune portal shows install success. Get-AppxPackage MSTeams shows InstallLocation is empty. Only occurs in China (zh-CN locale) environment, not Global.
rootCause: Windows 24H2 image includes built-in MSTeams v1.0 which does not include CN region. When teamsbootstrapper.exe -p upgrades the app, it retains original region settings. After Sysprep generalizes the image and User Locale changes to zh-CN, the region selection policy deletes Teams and Outlook due to sideloading policy restrictions because these packages lack CN region support.
solution: 1) Deprovision the built-in v1.0: teamsbootstrapper.exe -x. 2) Provision new Teams with offline MSIX: teamsbootstrapper.exe -p -o c:\path\to\teams.msix. This ensures proper region configuration. Do NOT just upgrade from built-in v1.0.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2510270030001930 _ After Teams install succeed, us.md

symptom: iOS Teams app fails MAM login with Conditional Access non-compliant error. Outlook/OneDrive/Word on same device work fine. Teams MAM logs show Failed to acquire token for AcquireTokenSilently with error Code: 2. ESTS shows ProtectionPolicyRequired (53005) or ExpiredOrRevokedGrantInactiveToken (700082). Teams is not listed as MAM-protected app.
rootCause: Teams caches a stale/old MAM OID from a previous enrollment period (e.g., user was intern, left company, re-joined). The old OID was wiped from Intune service but Teams still uses it for token acquisition. Outlook SDK (v21.1.0) handles OID migration correctly, but Teams SDK (v20.8.2) does not refresh the stale OID. upnCache shows 2 OIDs for same identity.
solution: 1) Uninstall and reinstall Teams to clear cached OID. 2) Long-term: Request Teams PG to update Intune MAM SDK version via ICM to match Outlook newer SDK. 3) Verify with MAM logs that upnCache entry count returns to 1 after fix.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/2510280030001846 - Teams unable to login.md

symptom: Edge Enterprise Mode deployed via Intune not working as expected on co-management (SCCM+Intune) device. Unexpected registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge created.
rootCause: SCCM CCM client creates Edge Enterprise Mode SiteList registry key to ensure Application Catalog website opens in IE instead of Edge. If no GPO exists for Edge Enterprise Mode, CCM client auto-creates the policy and XML file, overriding Intune-deployed settings.
solution: 1) Create registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\CCM with DWORD AllowConfigureMicrosoftEdge=0. 2) Delete HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\Main\EnterpriseMode\SiteList. 3) Deploy fix via Intune Win32 app with script.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/Classic Cases.md

symptom: Windows device fails to enroll in Intune as co-management device. Error 0x80180026 (MDM blocked) in event logs. SetManagedExternally registry value is set to 1.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments has SetManagedExternally set to 1, which blocks MDM enrollment. This can be left over from previous MDM configuration or set by Group Policy.
solution: Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments, change SetManagedExternally to 0.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/Classic Cases.md

symptom: Intune Disk Encryption (BitLocker) policy shows Error status for devices in Endpoint Security portal, but BitLocker is actually enabled and working. MDM event log shows FixedDrivesRecoveryOptions failed. Devices only have C: drive.
rootCause: BitLocker CSP FixedDrivesRecoveryOptions setting does not have a real Not Configured option. When admin selects Not Configured for Recovery key file creation, the CSP still sends the setting which fails on devices without a fixed data drive (only OS drive).
solution: Change Recovery key file creation under FixedDrivesRecoveryOptions from Not Configured to an explicit value: Allow, Require, or Disable. For BitLocker silent enablement, use Deny or Allow.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/Disk Encryption policy error.md

symptom: Co-management status cannot be enabled on SCCM + Intune enrolled device. CoManagementHandler.log shows Expected MDM_ConfigSetting instance is missing. WMI class MDM_ConfigSetting missing from root\cimv2\MDM namespace.
rootCause: Essential WMI class MDM_ConfigSetting under root\cimv2\MDM namespace is missing from the device OS. This class is required for co-management status evaluation. Also, SCCM 1806 had a bug where dates/months ending in zero caused server check-in time save failure.
solution: 1) Manually add MDM_ConfigSetting WMI class back to root\cimv2\MDM namespace. 2) Re-enroll device to MDM authority. 3) For SCCM 1806 date parsing bug, upgrade SCCM to newer version.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/Classic Cases.md

symptom: Co-management device deployed via Intune shows question mark in SCCM console instead of green tick. CMG-connected device shows error Cloud Management Gateway is not allowed to use on this machine. Packages deployed via SCCM not installed.
rootCause: Multiple CMG config issues: 1) Boundary configured with AD site instead of IP ranges, preventing internet-based clients from finding correct reference site systems (MP/DP/SUP). 2) Content distributed to on-premises DP instead of Cloud DP (CMG VM). 3) CA root/intermediate certificates not properly configured for CMG client connection.
solution: 1) Configure boundary with IP ranges instead of AD site for internet-based clients. 2) Distribute content to Cloud DP (CMG VM is cloud DP by default). 3) Reconfigure CA root/intermediate certificates for CMG connection.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/Classic Cases.md

symptom: iOS Teams login blocked by Conditional Access with error ProtectionPolicyRequired (53005). Teams not listed as MAM-protected app on device while Outlook/Edge are. MAM token acquisition fails with ExpiredOrRevokedGrantInactiveToken (700082) or error Code: 2. Multiple tenants affected.
rootCause: Teams MAM refresh token expired/revoked (due to password reset, prolonged inactivity, or stale OID). Teams cannot silently acquire new MAM token so it is not recognized as MAM-protected. CA policy requiring compliant application then blocks Teams. upnCache shows 2 OIDs for same identity indicating enrollment state corruption.
solution: 1) Uninstall and reinstall Teams to force fresh MAM enrollment. 2) If persists, check if password was recently reset. 3) For recurring cases, file IcM for Teams to update Intune SDK version. 4) Verify CA policy and Teams client ID in MAM resource config.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Mooncake Case Study/[To be organized]2506250030000735 IDO VW Teams.md

symptom: SCEP certificate profile fails to install on some iOS devices despite root CA and intermediate certificates being successful. iOS Xcode log shows 'The Registration Authority's response is invalid'. IIS log shows GetCACert requests but no GetCACaps requests.
rootCause: Device has an old root CA certificate from a previous MDM installation, likely restored via iOS backup/restore. The old root CA cert conflicts with the current root CA because the previous root CA cert's CRL is not accessible from the device.
solution: 1) On device: Settings > General > About > Certificate Trust Settings, disable the old root CA cert toggle. 2) Renew the root CA cert to include an externally accessible CRL endpoint. 3) Verify NDES cert bundle via: https://<ndes-server>/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll/getCaCert.p7b?operation=GetCACert&message=mstest
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## NDES_SCEP_PFX TSG/Intune_ SCEP certificate profile fails for some iO.md

symptom: NDES Connector related services (Intune Connector Service, PFX Certificate Connector) are stopped and cannot start. System Event log shows the service account used by NDES services is unable to log on as a service.
rootCause: The service account configured for NDES/Intune Connector services is not granted the 'Log on as a service' right in local security policy. This can happen after Group Policy updates or server migrations.
solution: Open Local Security Policy (secpol.msc) > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment > 'Log on as a service', add the NDES service account to the list. Then restart the NDES connector services.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## NDES_SCEP_PFX TSG/NDES Connector configure failed.md

symptom: NDES Connector related services are all stopped. System event log shows the NDES service account is unable to log on as a service. Connector UI cannot start properly.
rootCause: The service account used to run NDES-related services (Intune Connector Service etc.) does not have 'Log on as a service' right in local security policy. This can happen after security policy refresh, GPO change, or server migration.
solution: 1) Check System Event Log for 'unable to log on as service' error. 2) Open Local Security Policy (secpol.msc) > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment > Log on as a service. 3) Add the NDES service account to the policy. 4) Restart all NDES-related services. Also: When configuring connector, select China Cloud for 21v tenants and ensure IE browser supports JavaScript.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## NDES_SCEP_PFX TSG/NDES Connector configure failed.md

symptom: iOS/iPadOS User Enrollment method is not available in 21v (Mooncake) Intune; attempting to configure User Enrollment type results in no enrollment option or enrollment failure.
rootCause: User Enrollment (as a distinct Apple enrollment type using managed Apple ID) is not supported in the 21v (Mooncake) Intune environment due to feature gaps between global and China-operated Intune.
solution: Use BYOD enrollment via Company Portal or ADE (Automated Device Enrollment) instead of User Enrollment for iOS/iPadOS devices in 21v Intune. Do not configure User Enrollment type.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Readiness/Intune Readiness check list.md

symptom: BitLocker encryption policy deployed via Intune fails to apply on Azure VM used for lab testing; device shows non-compliant for disk encryption requirement despite policy being assigned.
rootCause: Azure VMs do not have TPM enabled by default. BitLocker encryption policies require Trusted Platform Module (TPM), and Azure VMs need virtual TPM (v-TPM) explicitly enabled in VM security settings.
solution: Enable v-TPM (virtual Trusted Platform Module) on the Azure VM before deploying BitLocker policies. Navigate to Azure Portal > VM > Settings > Security type > select Trusted launch > enable vTPM. Then re-deploy the BitLocker policy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Readiness/Intune Readiness check list/Retire - Readiness - wave 1.md

symptom: NDES/SCEP certificate deployment fails or encounters security issues when NDES role is installed on the same server as the Enterprise Certification Authority (CA); Intune Certificate Connector installation also not supported on the issuing CA server.
rootCause: Installing NDES on the same server as the issuing CA is an unsupported configuration that represents a security risk when the CA services internet requests. The connector installation is explicitly blocked or unsupported on the CA server.
solution: Install NDES server role and Intune Certificate Connector on a dedicated, separate server from the Enterprise CA. Never co-locate NDES with the issuing CA. Use a standalone member server for NDES.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Readiness/Intune Readiness check list/Retire - Readiness - wave 2.md

symptom: Intune Certificate Connector installation fails or does not complete properly; connector setup wizard errors out, services fail to register, or connector cannot communicate with Intune service after installation.
rootCause: IntuneCertificateConnector.exe requires elevated (administrator) privileges to properly install and register the connector services, configure IIS settings, and set up certificate handling. Running without elevation causes silent failures.
solution: Right-click IntuneCertificateConnector.exe and select 'Run as administrator' before starting the installation. Do not run the installer from a non-elevated command prompt or by double-clicking directly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Readiness/Intune Readiness check list/Retire - Readiness - wave 2.md

symptom: Cannot access Intune Kusto cluster for Mooncake (intunecn.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn) despite having IntuneKusto-CSS entitlement; Kusto queries fail with permission denied error when troubleshooting 21v tenant issues.
rootCause: IntuneKusto-CSS entitlement only grants access to Global Intune Kusto clusters, not the Mooncake (China) Kusto cluster. A separate entitlement 'IntuneKusto-CSSMooncake' is required specifically for China cloud Kusto access.
solution: Request the 'IntuneKusto-CSSMooncake' entitlement from CoreIdentity portal (https://coreidentity.microsoft.com/manage/Entitlement/entitlement/intunekustoc-ftdh). Both IntuneKusto-CSS (Global) and IntuneKusto-CSSMooncake (China) are needed for full coverage.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Readiness/Permission Request.md

symptom: ADMX-backed custom OMA-URI policy deployed via Intune shows 'Not applicable' or fails on Windows Professional edition devices, while succeeding on Enterprise edition.
rootCause: Certain Policy CSPs (e.g., AppVirtualization) used in ADMX-backed policies are only supported on Windows Enterprise/Education editions. Professional edition does not support these CSPs.
solution: Verify CSP edition support at docs.microsoft.com/windows/client-management/mdm/policy-csp-* before deploying. Use GPSearch tool (gpsearch.azurewebsites.net) to check CSP applicability. Target policy only to Enterprise/Education devices using assignment filters.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\ADMX backed policy.md

symptom: Intune Endpoint Security App Control for Business policy returns error 65000 when deploying a template generated by WDAC Wizard; the same template works via Group Policy.
rootCause: WDAC Wizard-generated XML template contains <PolicyTypeID> but lacks <PolicyID>. Intune requires <PolicyID> to generate the {policy_GUID}.CIP file under C:\windows\system32\codeintegrity\CIPolicies\Active\. Without it, Intune cannot create the CIP file and returns error 65000. Group Policy uses a different format (SiPolicy.p7b) and does not require PolicyID.
solution: Edit the WDAC XML template: (1) Remove <PolicyTypeID> element, (2) Add <PolicyID> with a unique GUID (generate via PowerShell: New-Guid), (3) Set <BasePolicyID> to the same GUID if no base policy exists, (4) Re-upload to Intune. Verify by checking if {GUID}.CIP file appears in C:\windows\system32\codeintegrity\CIPolicies\Active\.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\Application Control for business (preview) trouble.md

symptom: Autopilot V2 (Windows Device Preparation) enrollment fails in China mainland due to network connectivity issues; official Microsoft documentation does not include the complete URL whitelist required for 21Vianet.
rootCause: The official Intune China endpoints documentation is incomplete. Autopilot V2 enrollment requires additional URLs (e.g., clientconfig.passport.net, config.edge.skype.com, acctcdn.msftauth.net, account.live.com, login.microsoftonline.com, acctcdn.msauth.net) that are not listed in the official docs.
solution: Whitelist required URLs: clientconfig.passport.net, config.edge.skype.com, www.msftconnecttest.com, dns.msftncsi.com, acctcdn.msftauth.net, portal.azure.cn, portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn, portal.manage.microsoftonline.cn, intune.microsoftonline.cn, syncservice.partner.microsoftonline.cn, login.chinacloudapi.cn, graph.chinacloudapi.cn, microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn, enterpriseregistration.partner.microsoftonline.cn, account.live.com, login.microsoftonline.com, acctcdn.msauth.net. Optional: ztd.dds.microsoft.com, cs.dds.microsoft.com, login.live.com. Use PowerShell Test-NetConnection script to validate ports 80/443.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\Autopilot V2 TSG - Windows Device Preparation\21v China autopilot v2 network requirement_URLs wh.md

symptom: Autopilot ESP (Enrollment Status Page) fails with error 0x800705b4 (E_TIMEOUT) at 'Preparing device for mobile management' in China mainland. IME or Win32 app download fails during ESP.
rootCause: Network connectivity issues to CDN endpoints in China prevent IME MSI or Win32 app download from completing within timeout (30 min for IME, 60 min per Win32 app). CDN URL depends on tenant Azure Scale Unit location, not device location. China cloud CDN: sovereignprodimedatapri/sec/hotfix.azureedge.net.
solution: 1) Get IME download URL from registry; 2) Test CDN download manually in browser from same network; 3) Use psping -t <CDN_URL>:443 to test TCP; 4) Use tracert to check routing; 5) Use dnschecker.org if IP looks abnormal; 6) For Win32: reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Autopilot\EnrollmentStatusTracking\ESPTrackingInfo\Diagnostics\Sidecar /s (3=success,4=failure); 7) Check IME log for download URL and progress.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\Autopilot V2 TSG - Windows Device Preparation\Enrollment Status Page Troubleshooting ESP - Faile.md

symptom: MAM (Mobile Application Management) for Edge on Windows fails when device is Azure AD joined (AADJ) or Hybrid Azure AD joined (HAADJ). Conditional Access with 'Require app protection policy' blocks access.
rootCause: Desktop MAM for Edge on Windows only supports Azure AD registered (Workplace Joined) devices. AADJ and HAADJ use MDM/device-wide management which conflicts with MAM. Max 3 users can WPJ from the same BYOD device across all tenants. 'Allow my organization to manage my device' must be unchecked during registration.
solution: 1) Ensure device Join Type is 'Azure AD registered'; 2) Uncheck 'Allow my organization to manage my device' during registration; 3) Verify MAM flags via edge://edge-dlp-internals (mslrmv2=Enabled, msMamDlp=Enabled); 4) If flags don't persist: msedge --enable-features=msDesktopMam; 5) Check mamlog.txt at %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\; 6) Requirements: Win11 22H2+, Edge v115+, Intune license, Entra ID P1.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\App protection policy for Edge on Windows.md

symptom: Autopilot V2 enrollment fails with error 80180014 when personal device enrollment is blocked under enrollment restrictions. Corporate device identifier was recently uploaded but not yet recognized.
rootCause: Corporate device identifiers take time to propagate after CSV upload. If enrollment is attempted before propagation completes and enrollment restrictions block personal devices, enrollment is rejected with error 80180014.
solution: Wait for corporate device identifiers to propagate after CSV upload. Verify in Intune portal: Devices > Enrollment > Corporate device identifiers. To create CSV: Get-WmiObject Win32_ComputerSystem | FL Manufacturer, Model; Get-WmiObject Win32_BIOS | FL SerialNumber. Format: Manufacturer,Model,SerialNumber.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\Autopilot V2 TSG - Windows Device Preparation\How to Setup Autopilot V2.md

symptom: Autopilot V2 Enrollment Time Grouping (ETG) fails to automatically add device to security group during enrollment in 21Vianet (China) environment.
rootCause: The Intune Provisioning Client service principal (AppId: f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c) must be manually registered in 21v tenant and set as owner of the device security group. Unlike global cloud, this SP is not auto-provisioned in 21v.
solution: Run: Install-Module AzureAD; Connect-AzureAD -AzureEnvironmentName AzureChinaCloud; New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId f1346770-5b25-470b-88bd-d5744ab7952c. Then create security group with 'Intune Provisioning Client' as owner. Use intune.microsoftonline.cn portal for 21v.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\## Windows TSG\Autopilot V2 TSG - Windows Device Preparation\How to Setup Autopilot V2.md

symptom: Intune Endpoint Security firewall rule deployment fails with error 0x80070057 (E_INVALIDARG); one bad rule in an Atomic SyncML block causes all subsequent rules in the same policy to be blocked.
rootCause: Common causes: 1) Invalid file path with typos in environment variables (e.g., extra space in %ProgramFiles(x86)%), custom variables, or forward slashes instead of backslashes. 2) Invalid port ranges (e.g., descending range 65535-65534). 3) Missing TCP/UDP protocol when port ranges are configured. The Atomic block structure means one bad rule blocks all subsequent rules.
solution: 1) Identify the failing rule via Event Log EventID 404 under DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider. 2) Check registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\Mdm\FirewallRules for created rules. 3) Use Test-IntuneFirewallRules PowerShell script (https://github.com/markstan/Test-IntuneFirewallRules) to identify bad rules. 4) Use SyncML Viewer to trace individual rule creation. 5) Fix the bad rule parameters and redeploy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/How to trace and troubleshoot the Intune Endpoint.md

symptom: INF driver installation via Intune Win32 app deployment using pnputil.exe in PowerShell script fails; driver does not install on target devices despite IME processing the app.
rootCause: Standard INF driver install methods (pnputil.exe invoked via PowerShell Start-Process) do not work reliably through Intune Win32 app deployment pipeline. The execution context and timing of the IME process can prevent pnputil from completing properly.
solution: Check pnputil results in %windir%\inf\setupapi.dev.log. Note that Intune downloads app content to C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Intune Management Extension\Content, copies to C:\windows\IMECache, and deletes both after install attempt. Check IME logs at C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\IntuneManagementExtension\Logs. Consider alternative driver deployment methods (Windows Update for Business, driver update policies).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/INF driver install.md

symptom: Need to verify TattooRemoval is running correctly for a device; or policy settings not being cleaned up after user disabled/deleted.
rootCause: TattooRemoval process may not have run yet due to hold-off period, or need to confirm it was triggered. When a user is disabled or deleted, service auto-detects and sends TattooRemoval command to remove all user-targeted policies.
solution: Kusto: IntuneEvent | where AccountId == '<tenantId>' | where * has 'Tattoo' — look for DMSPolicyProcessor 'Starting tattoo removal process with previously remediated=X and current get=Y'. DeviceManagementProvider | where ActivityId contains '<deviceId>' | where message contains 'removal' — shows timing. For disabled/deleted users: service auto-sends TattooRemoval with userId=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, removing all user-targeted policy instances.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Intune policy TattooRemoval_.md

symptom: Co-managed devices show duplicate entries in Intune portal -- one as normal Intune entry, second as ConfigMgr record from tenant attach upload.
rootCause: Devices are enrolled to Intune via co-management AND uploaded by tenant attach simultaneously. The records are supposed to merge automatically but sometimes this takes longer than usual, resulting in persistent duplicates.
solution: On affected device: HKLM\SOFTWARE\MICROSOFT\CCM create REG_DWORD CoMgmtReportForceSend=1. Restart CCMExec (SMS Agent Host) service. Restart device. Wait for duplicate to be removed. Set key back to 0 or delete it.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/Co-management/Comanaged Devices Duplicate Entry On Intune Portal.md

symptom: Endpoint Protection tattooed settings (ExcludedExtensions, ExcludedPaths, ExcludedProcesses) not removed or revert to previous values for co-managed devices when Device Configuration workload is set to SCCM.
rootCause: Known issue with co-management EP tattoo behavior -- when Device Configuration workload is managed by ConfigMgr, Intune-applied Defender exclusion settings persist in registry (tattooed) and are not cleaned up.
solution: Run cleanup script to delete tattooed registry keys: delete ExcludedExtensions/ExcludedPaths/ExcludedProcesses from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\Providers (all subkeys) and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Policy Manager.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/Co-management/Comanagement Workloads New values.md

symptom: Configuration Manager agent state shows unhealthy/unknown/installed-but-not-assigned in Intune portal for co-managed devices, even when device appears healthy locally.
rootCause: Intune evaluates ConfigMgr agent state via ClientHealthStatus and ClientHealthLastSyncTime in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\MDM. Healthy=7 (bitmask: Installed+Registered+Healthy). If Compliance workload is managed by ConfigMgr, device will not sync time to Intune (portal shows 2/1/1900). Registry value 6 (missing Healthy bit) causes unknown status.
solution: Check CcmNotificationAgent.log for BGB sync issues. If ClientHealthLastSyncTime is current but portal shows 2/1/1900 then switch Compliance policies workload to Intune or Pilot Intune. If ClientHealthStatus != 7, investigate client health (8=install error, 16=MP communication error). Thresholds: healthy(7) tolerates 30 days stale; unhealthy tolerates only 48 hours.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/Co-management/Configuration Manager agent state in Intune Portal.md

symptom: GPO-based auto-enrollment into Intune fails for co-managed hybrid AAD joined devices; dsregcmd /status shows AzureAdPrt: NO; device cannot obtain Primary Refresh Token.
rootCause: User UPN suffix in Active Directory is non-routable (e.g., .local domain), preventing the user from obtaining the AzureADPrt token. The PRT is required for GPO-based auto-enrollment. SAM account format works but UPN must be routable.
solution: In Active Directory Users and Computers open user properties Account tab, modify UPN suffix from non-routable (e.g., wmt.local) to routable domain (e.g., company.com). Verify with whoami /upn and dsregcmd /status (AzureAdPrt should show YES).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/Co-management/Admin Experience.md

symptom: Question whether GPO Enable automatic MDM enrollment using default Azure AD credentials is required for co-management MDM enrollment of hybrid AAD joined devices.
rootCause: Without GPO, co-management queues an enrollment timer with randomization delay (can be hours). MDM enrollment only proceeds after timer fires AND an Intune-licensed user signs in to obtain AAD info. With GPO, the scheduled task triggers MDM enrollment immediately upon GPO application, bypassing the ConfigMgr timer.
solution: GPO is NOT required for co-management auto-enrollment. Without GPO: ConfigMgr CoManagementHandler queues enrollment timer, waits for user logon, enrolls when timer fires. With GPO: enrollment triggers immediately via scheduled task. Use GPO when faster enrollment is desired. Both methods result in the same co-managed state. Log: CoManagementHandler.log shows timer scheduling and enrollment flow.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\Co-management\Question 2_ (Auto-Enroll VS GPO)Do we need to depl.md

symptom: Question whether SCCM can still deploy applications to co-managed devices after switching Client Apps workload to Intune.
rootCause: Switching Client Apps workload to Intune enables Intune app deployment but does not disable SCCM app deployment. Both channels remain functional simultaneously.
solution: After switching Client Apps workload to Intune: 1) Intune-deployed available apps appear in Company Portal. 2) SCCM-deployed available apps still appear in Software Center. 3) SCCM required deployments still auto-install via Software Center. Both management channels can deploy apps concurrently to co-managed devices.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\Co-management\Question 4_ After client app workload to Intune, c.md

symptom: After switching Device Configuration workload to Intune in co-management, Resource Access and Endpoint Protection workloads are also auto-moved to Intune and cannot be switched back to ConfigMgr independently.
rootCause: By design, Device Configuration workload has a dependency on Resource Access and Endpoint Protection workloads. When Device Configuration is moved to Intune, the other two are automatically included.
solution: This is expected behavior. When switching Device Configuration workload to Intune, Resource Access and Endpoint Protection will auto-move as well. To keep Resource Access or Endpoint Protection under ConfigMgr, Device Configuration must also remain under ConfigMgr. Plan workload migration accordingly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\Co-management\Workloads Related _ Comanagement capbilities.md

symptom: Co-managed device shows See ConfigMgr for compliance status in Intune portal and compliance policy deployment fails for the device.
rootCause: Compliance Policies workload is still managed by ConfigMgr (not switched to Intune). When the workload is under CM authority, Intune cannot evaluate compliance and shows See ConfigMgr status.
solution: Switch the Compliance Policies workload from ConfigMgr to Intune (or Pilot Intune). After switching, the device will report Compliant/Non-compliant status in Intune portal, and Intune compliance policies will be evaluated successfully.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook\POD\VMSCIM\4. Services\Intune\Co-management\Workloads Related _ Comanagement capbilities.md
